papers.title: DFVG: A Heterogeneous Architecture for Speculative Decoding with Draft-on-FPGA and Verify-on-GPU.
papers.abstract: Speculative decoding is a promising paradigm that accelerates LLM inference by generating drafts and performing verification. However, such systems still face three major challenges: (1) The imbalance in resource requirements between draft and verification models result in low utilization and energy inefficiency when deployed together. (2) Fixed-pattern token trees produce many candidates but few valid paths, resulting in redundant drafts due to the lack of full leverage of the inherent confidence in dynamic generation. (3) Asynchronous execution with frequent alternation between the two stages suffers from idle waiting and rollback overhead. To address these issues, we propose DFVG , a heterogeneous speculative decoding architecture that offloads draft generation to FPGAs and verification to GPUs, exploiting their complementary strengths. We introduce three key contributions: (1) Heterogeneous architecture design that partitions speculative decoding into FPGA-based drafting and GPU-based verification, exploiting complementary hardware strengths with an overlap processor for high-throughput execution; (2) Hardware-aware dynamic draft generation that dynamically predicts speculative branches and token lengths based on model confidence while considering hardware parallelism limits; (3) Tightly-coupled heterogeneous pipeline with stagedecoupled scheduling that allocates execution windows between stages, combined with lightweight cross-device alignment and rollback prediction strategies. Comprehensive evaluation on mainstream models (OPT, LLaMA, Qwen) demonstrates DFVG achieves up to 3.26× speedup and 5.8× energy efficiency improvement over existing approaches. The source code at: https://github.com/ShaoqiangLu/DFVG
papers.abstract_zh: 推测性解码是一种很有前途的范例，它通过生成草稿和执行验证来加速 LLM 推理。然而，此类系统仍面临三大挑战：（1）草稿模型和验证模型之间的资源需求不平衡，导致部署在一起时利用率低、能源效率低。 (2) 固定模式令牌树产生许多候选路径，但有效路径很少，由于缺乏充分利用动态生成的固有置信度，导致冗余草稿。 (3) 两个阶段之间频繁交替的异步执行会遭受空闲等待和回滚开销。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了 DFVG，这是一种异构推测解码架构，可将草稿生成任务转移到 FPGA，将验证任务转移到 GPU，从而充分利用它们的互补优势。我们介绍了三个关键贡献：（1）异构架构设计，将推测解码划分为基于 FPGA 的绘图和基于 GPU 的验证，利用重叠处理器的互补硬件优势来实现高吞吐量执行； (2) 硬件感知的动态草稿生成，根据模型置信度动态预测推测分支和令牌长度，同时考虑硬件并行度限制； (3)紧耦合异构管道，具有阶段解耦调度，在阶段之间分配执行窗口，并结合轻量级跨设备对齐和回滚预测策略。对主流模型（OPT、LLaMA、Qwen）的综合评估表明，DFVG 较现有方法实现了高达 3.26 倍的加速和 5.8 倍的能效提升。源代码位于：https://github.com/ShaoqiangLu/DFVG

papers.title: DIP: Efficient Large Multimodal Model Training with Dynamic Interleaved Pipeline.
papers.abstract:
Large multimodal models (LMMs) have demonstrated excellent capabilities in both understanding and generation tasks with various modalities. While these models can accept flexible combinations of input data, their training efficiency suffers from two major issues: pipeline stage imbalance caused by heterogeneous model architectures, and training data dynamicity stemming from the diversity of multimodal data.

In this paper, we present DIP, a dynamic and modality-aware pipeline scheduling framework designed for LMM training. DIP tackles the challenge of dynamic imbalance via two key techniques: (1) separating computations of different modalities into dedicated pipeline segments to balance workloads within a continuous set of stages; (2) dynamically splitting input data into finer-grained, modality-specific sub-microbatches to balance workloads across these segments. By asynchronously generating pipeline schedules on idle CPU resources during training, DIP dynamically tailors stage executions to each input batch without stalling the training process. We validate DIP on a diverse set of five LMMs, ranging from 12B to 94B parameters and including vision-language and diffusion models. Experimental results show that our system achieves up to 97.3% higher throughput compared to state-of-the-art systems, demonstrating strong adaptability to fluctuating multimodal training workloads.
papers.abstract_zh:
大型多模态模型（LMM）在理解和生成各种模态任务方面表现出了出色的能力。虽然这些模型可以接受输入数据的灵活组合，但它们的训练效率受到两个主要问题的影响：异构模型架构导致的管道阶段不平衡，以及多模态数据多样性导致的训练数据动态性。

在本文中，我们提出了 DIP，一种专为 LMM 训练而设计的动态且模态感知的管道调度框架。 DIP 通过两项关键技术解决动态不平衡的挑战：（1）将不同模态的计算分离到专用的管道段中，以平衡一组连续阶段内的工作负载； (2) 将输入数据动态分割为更细粒度、特定于模态的子微批次，以平衡这些部分之间的工作负载。通过在训练期间在空闲 CPU 资源上异步生成管道调度，DIP 可以根据每个输入批次动态定制阶段执行，而不会停止训练过程。我们在五个 LMM 的不同集合上验证 DIP，参数范围从 12B 到 94B，包括视觉语言和扩散模型。实验结果表明，与最先进的系统相比，我们的系统吞吐量提高了 97.3%，表现出对波动的多模式训练工作负载的强大适应性。

papers.title: Dynamic Sparsity in Large-Scale Video DiT Training.
papers.abstract:
Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) have shown remarkable performance in generating high-quality videos. However, the quadratic complexity of 3D full attention remains a bottleneck in scaling DiT training, especially with high-definition, lengthy videos, where it can consume up to 95% of processing time and demand specialized context parallelism.

This paper introduces DSV to accelerate video DiT training by leveraging the dynamic attention sparsity we empirically observe. DSV uses a two-stage algorithm to capture the dynamic sparsity patterns via low-rank based approximation of the original query and key. It employs custom kernels to efficiently identify critical key-value pairs and compute the sparse attention. To accommodate the new sparsity dimension, DSV adopts a hybrid sparsity-aware context parallelism that re-balances the skewed workload across attention heads and blocks due to sparsity heterogeneity. DSV achieves up to 3.02x higher training throughput, scaling to 128 GPUs and 520k token lengths, while maintaining model quality comparable to full attention schemes.
papers.abstract_zh:
扩散变压器 (DiT) 在生成高质量视频方面表现出了卓越的性能。然而，3D 全注意力的二次复杂度仍然是扩展 DiT 训练的瓶颈，特别是对于高清、长视频，它可能会消耗高达 95% 的处理时间，并需要专门的上下文并行性。

本文引入 DSV，利用我们凭经验观察到的动态注意力稀疏性来加速视频 DiT 训练。 DSV 使用两阶段算法通过原始查询和密钥的基于低秩的近似来捕获动态稀疏模式。它采用自定义内核来有效识别关键键值对并计算稀疏注意力。为了适应新的稀疏性维度，DSV 采用了混合稀疏性感知上下文并行性，可以重新平衡由于稀疏性异质性而导致的注意力头和块之间的倾斜工作负载。 DSV 的训练吞吐量提高了 3.02 倍，可扩展到 128 个 GPU 和 520k 令牌长度，同时保持与全注意力方案相当的模型质量。

papers.title: EARTH: An Efficient MoE Accelerator with Entropy-Aware Speculative Prefetch and Result Reuse.
papers.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models significantly reduce computation in large language models by activating only a subset of experts per input token, but they introduce severe memory bottlenecks due to the large number of expert parameters. Existing offloading and prefetching strategies either incur accuracy loss, prohibitively high memory traffic, or high decoding overhead, limiting deployment on resource-constrained hardware. In this work, we present EARTH, a hardware–software co-design that addresses these challenges through three key innovations. First, we propose a dual-entropy encoding scheme that decomposes each expert into a high-information base and a delta component, enabling compact storage while preserving accuracy via adaptive precision management. Second, we introduce a delta-aware speculative prefetching and reuse mechanism that preloads base components of predicted experts and selectively fetches deltas, reusing previously computed delta patterns to reduce memory traffic and redundant computation. Third, we design a hardware accelerator that is co-designed to efficiently support this encoding and prefetching strategy, optimizing execution order, parallelism, and memory utilization. Across representative MoE workloads, EARTH reduces data movement overhead, improves prefetch efficiency, and achieves up to 2.10× speedup compared to state-of-the-art baselines, while maintaining high model accuracy.
papers.abstract_zh: 专家混合 (MoE) 模型通过仅激活每个输入标记的专家子集，显着减少了大型语言模型中的计算，但由于大量的专家参数，它们引入了严重的内存瓶颈。现有的卸载和预取策略要么会导致准确性损失、内存流量过高，要么解码开销较高，从而限制了在资源受限的硬件上的部署。在这项工作中，我们提出了 EARTH，这是一种硬件软件协同设计，通过三项关键创新来应对这些挑战。首先，我们提出了一种双熵编码方案，将每个专家分解为高信息库和增量组件，从而实现紧凑存储，同时通过自适应精度管理保持准确性。其次，我们引入了一种增量感知推测预取和重用机制，该机制预加载预测专家的基本组件并有选择地获取增量，重用先前计算的增量模式以减少内存流量和冗余计算。第三，我们设计了一个硬件加速器，旨在有效支持这种编码和预取策略，优化执行顺序、并行性和内存利用率。在具有代表性的 MoE 工作负载中，EARTH 减少了数据移动开销，提高了预取效率，与最先进的基准相比，速度提高了 2.10 倍，同时保持了较高的模型精度。

papers.title: Efficient Remote Memory Ordering for Non-Coherent Systems.
papers.abstract: Software using non-coherent interconnects like PCI Express requires fine-grained memory ordering, but current hardware mandates the use of costly source-side serialization. We show that this architectural mismatch severely limits the performance of two critical applications: (1) the transmission of network packets from a CPU to a NIC (requiring write-to-write ordering) and (2) key-value store lookups by an RDMA-enabled NIC (requiring read-to-read ordering). We address this by proposing a new destination-based ordering model and the hardware-software co-design comprising PCIe extensions and ISA extensions that allow software to express ordering intent efficiently. Novel microarchitecture at the Root Complex enforces these expressed semantics, eliminating source-side stalls. Our approach significantly improves the throughput of these application kernels and enables new, simpler protocols that outperform the state-of-the-art.
papers.abstract_zh: 使用 PCI Express 等非一致性互连的软件需要细粒度的内存排序，但当前的硬件要求使用昂贵的源端序列化。我们表明，这种架构不匹配严重限制了两个关键应用程序的性能：(1) 将网络数据包从 CPU 传输到 NIC（需要写入顺序）和 (2) 支持 RDMA 的 NIC 进行键值存储查找（需要读取顺序）。我们通过提出一种新的基于目的地的订购模型和包括 PCIe 扩展和 ISA 扩展的软硬件协同设计来解决这个问题，使软件能够有效地表达订购意图。根联合体的新颖微架构强制执行这些表达的语义，消除源端停顿。我们的方法显着提高了这些应用程序内核的吞吐量，并支持新的、更简单的协议，其性能优于最先进的协议。

papers.title: Efficient Temporal Graph Network Training via Unified Redundancy Elimination.
papers.abstract: Temporal Graph Network (TGN) is increasingly adopted to model evolving relationships in dynamic graphs. However, the training pipeline is plagued by pervasive redundancy in computation, storage, and data loading. These redundancies harm computational efficiency, exacerbate memory pressure, and induce excessive CPU-GPU data transfers. We present PULSE, an end-to-end TGN training framework that systematically eliminates redundancies guided by a unified minimal-unit principle. To realize such principle, PULSE defines three synergetic units: 1) the Minimal Input Unit (MIU) for component-wise deduplication and operator-level reconstruction of redundant computations, 2) the Minimal Storage Unit (MSU) for dependency-guided message reconstruction, only preserving irreproducible entries while enabling on-demand recovery of others, and 3) the Minimal Reuse Unit (MRU) for GPU memory management, combining a BlockPool-based buffer allocator with a bipartite temporal reuse strategy to mitigate fragmentation and exploit inter-batch locality. Experimental results on representative benchmarks demonstrate that PULSE improves training throughput by up to 6.67× over the state-of-the-art baselines.
papers.abstract_zh: 时态图网络（TGN）越来越多地用于模拟动态图中不断变化的关系。然而，训练管道受到计算、存储和数据加载中普遍冗余的困扰。这些冗余会损害计算效率，加剧内存压力，并导致过多的 CPU-GPU 数据传输。我们提出了 PULSE，一个端到端的 TGN 训练框架，它在统一的最小单元原则的指导下系统地消除了冗余。为了实现这一原则，PULSE 定义了三个协同单元：1）用于组件级重复数据删除和冗余计算的操作级重建的最小输入单元（MIU），2）用于依赖引导消息重建的最小存储单元（MSU），仅保留不可重复的条目，同时启用其他条目的按需恢复，以及 3）用于 GPU 内存管理的最小重用单元（MRU），将基于 BlockPool 的缓冲区分配器与双向时间重用相结合减少碎片和利用批次间局部性的策略。代表性基准的实验结果表明，与最先进的基准相比，PULSE 将训练吞吐量提高了 6.67 倍。

papers.title: Enabling Fast Networking in the Public Cloud.
papers.abstract: Despite a decade of research, most high-performance userspace network stacks remain impractical for public cloud tenants developing their applications atop Virtual Machines (VMs). We identify two root causes: (1) reliance on specialized NIC features (e.g., flow steering, deep buffers) absent in commodity cloud vNICs, and (2) rigid execution models ill-suited to diverse application needs. We present Machnet, a highperformance and flexible userspace network stack designed for public cloud VMs. Machnet uses only a minimal set of vNIC features that any major cloud provider supports. It also relies on a microkernel architecture to enable flexible application execution. We evaluate Machnet across three major public clouds and on production-grade applications, including a key-value store, an HTTP server, and a statemachine replication system. We release Machnet at https: //github.com/microsoft/machnet.
papers.abstract_zh: 尽管经过了十年的研究，大多数高性能用户空间网络堆栈对于在虚拟机 (VM) 上开发应用程序的公共云租户来说仍然不切实际。我们确定了两个根本原因：(1) 依赖于商用云 vNIC 中缺乏的专用 NIC 功能（例如流量控制、深度缓冲区），以及 (2) 僵化的执行模型不适合不同的应用程序需求。我们推出了 Machnet，这是一种专为公共云虚拟机设计的高性能且灵活的用户空间网络堆栈。 Machnet 仅使用任何主要云提供商都支持的最小 vNIC 功能集。它还依赖于微内核架构来实现灵活的应用程序执行。我们跨三个主要公共云和生产级应用程序评估了 Machnet，包括键值存储、HTTP 服务器和状态机复制系统。我们在 https://github.com/microsoft/machnet 发布了 Machnet。

papers.title: Evaluating Compiler Optimization Impacts on zkVM Performance.
papers.abstract:
Zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs) are the cornerstone of programmable cryptography. They enable (1) privacy-preserving and verifiable computation across blockchains, and (2) an expanding range of off-chain applications such as credential schemes. Zero-knowledge virtual machines (zkVMs) lower the barrier by turning ZKPs into a drop-in backend for standard compilation pipelines. This lets developers write proof-generating programs in conventional languages (e.g., Rust or C++) instead of hand-crafting arithmetic circuits. However, these VMs inherit compiler infrastructures tuned for traditional architectures rather than for proof systems. In particular, standard compiler optimizations assume features that are absent in zkVMs, including cache locality, branch prediction, or instruction-level parallelism. Therefore, their impact on proof generation is questionable.

We present the first systematic study of the impact of compiler optimizations on zkVMs. We evaluate 64 LLVM passes, six standard optimization levels, and an unoptimized baseline across 58 benchmarks on two RISC-V–based zkVMs (RISC Zero and SP1). While standard LLVM optimization levels do improve zkVM performance (over 40%), their impact is far smaller than on traditional CPUs, since their decisions rely on hardware features rather than proof constraints. Guided by a performance impact analysis, we slightly refine a small set of LLVM passes to be zkVM-aware, improving zkVM execution time by up to 45% (average +4.6% on RISC Zero, +1% on SP1) and achieving consistent proving-time gains. Our work highlights the potential of compiler-level optimizations for zkVM performance and opens new directions for zkVM-specific passes, backends, and superoptimizers.
papers.abstract_zh:
零知识证明（ZKP）是可编程密码学的基石。它们支持（1）跨区块链的隐私保护和可验证计算，以及（2）不断扩大的链下应用程序，例如凭证方案。零知识虚拟机 (zkVM) 通过将 ZKP 转变为标准编译管道的嵌入式后端来降低障碍。这使得开发人员可以用传统语言（例如 Rust 或 C++）编写证明生成程序，而不是手工制作算术电路。然而，这些虚拟机继承了针对传统架构而不是证明系统调整的编译器基础设施。特别是，标准编译器优化假设了 zkVM 中不存在的功能，包括缓存局部性、分支预测或指令级并行性。因此，它们对证明生成的影响值得怀疑。

我们首次系统地研究了编译器优化对 zkVM 的影响。我们在两个基于 RISC-V 的 zkVM（RISC Zero 和 SP1）上跨 58 个基准评估了 64 个 LLVM 遍、六个标准优化级别和一个未优化的基线。虽然标准 LLVM 优化级别确实提高了 zkVM 性能（超过 40%），但其影响远小于传统 CPU，因为它们的决策依赖于硬件功能而不是证明约束。在性能影响分析的指导下，我们稍微改进了一小部分 LLVM 通道，使其具有 zkVM 感知能力，将 zkVM 执行时间提高了 45%（RI​​SC Zero 上平均 +4​​.6%，SP1 上平均 +1%），并实现了一致的证明时间增益。我们的工作强调了 zkVM 性能的编译器级优化的潜力，并为 zkVM 特定的通道、后端和超级优化器开辟了新的方向。

papers.title: Falcon: Algorithm-Hardware Co-Design for Efficient Fully Homomorphic Encryption Accelerator.
papers.abstract:
Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) enables computation on encrypted data without compromising privacy, positioning it as a promising solution for secure cloud computing. However, its substantial computational overhead impedes practical deployment, prompting the development of dedicated hardware accelerators. In practice, when deploying cryptographic algorithm optimizations on FHE accelerators, hardware constraints typically such as limited memory capacity, often lead to a disparity between theoretical algorithmic advantage and achievable hardware efficiency.

We present Falcon, an algorithm-hardware co-design for efficient FHE acceleration. We first conduct computational analysis of the prevalent minimum key-switching method, and propose hardware-oriented algorithmic optimizations that substantially reduce computational overhead. We then introduce further refinements to decrease the inherent inter-cluster communication overhead in multi-cluster architectures, while preserving computational advantages. By characterizing the arithmetic patterns intrinsic to primary primitives, we devise hardware-specific arithmetic fusion and functional reuse across computational units. Finally, we present a memory-adaptive strategy to effectively deploy Falcon to prior accelerators under constrained on-chip memory budgets. Experimental results demonstrate that applying our algorithm–hardware co-design delivers up to a 1.48× speedup with a negligible area overhead (only a 0.8% increase).
papers.abstract_zh:
完全同态加密 (FHE) 可以在不损害隐私的情况下对加密数据进行计算，使其成为安全云计算的一种有前景的解决方案。然而，其大量的计算开销阻碍了实际部署，从而促进了专用硬件加速器的开发。在实践中，当在 FHE 加速器上部署加密算法优化时，硬件限制（例如有限的内存容量）通常会导致理论算法优势与可实现的硬件效率之间的差异。

我们推出了 Falcon，一种算法-硬件协同设计，可实现高效 FHE 加速。我们首先对流行的最小按键切换方法进行计算分析，并提出面向硬件的算法优化，从而大大减少计算开销。然后，我们引入进一步的改进，以减少多集群架构中固有的集群间通信开销，同时保留计算优势。通过表征主要基元固有的算术模式，我们设计了特定于硬件的算术融合和跨计算单元的功能重用。最后，我们提出了一种内存自适应策略，可以在片上内存预算有限的情况下有效地将 Falcon 部署到先前的加速器上。实验结果表明，应用我们的算法-硬件协同设计可实现高达 1.48 倍的加速，而面积开销可以忽略不计（仅增加 0.8%）。

papers.title: FastTTS: Accelerating Test-Time Scaling for Edge LLM Reasoning.
papers.abstract: Recent advances in reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) are driving the emergence of agentic AI systems. Edge deployment of LLM agents near end users is increasingly necessary to protect data privacy, enable offline use, and provide responsive interaction with local context. However, strict memory constraints on edge devices limit deployment to smaller LLMs, whose reasoning capabilities are much weaker than those of large cloud models, hindering practical deployment of edge agentic AI. Test-Time Scaling (TTS) offers a promising solution by allocating more compute during inference to enhance the reasoning capability of edge LLMs. However, current TTS methods introduce heavy hardware performance overhead on resource-constrained devices, making them impractical for real applications. To address this challenge, we present FastTTS, a serving system that enables fast and efficient TTS for memory-constrained LLM reasoning. After analyzing common patterns across various TTS methods and identifying their performance bottlenecks, we introduce three novel techniques: i) Speculative Beam Extension, which mitigates system stragglers caused by irregular reasoning paths, ii) Asymmetric Multi-Model Memory Allocation, which dynamically balances memory usage between token generation and reasoning-step verification, and iii) Dynamic Prefix-Aware Scheduling, which optimizes reasoning execution to maximize KV-cache reuse across search paths. FastTTS offers a plug-and-play third-party library on top of vLLM, enabling edge LLMs (łeq 7B) on a single consumer GPU (24 GB) to match cloud-model accuracy and cloud-measured latency. Comprehensive evaluation shows that FastTTS achieves an average 2.2× higher goodput and reduces latency by 38%-68% compared to the vLLM baseline; it pushes the boundaries of low-latency Test-Time Scaling on memory-constrained edge devices and highlights the potential for democratizing agentic AI.
papers.abstract_zh: 推理大型语言模型 (LLM) 的最新进展正在推动代理人工智能系统的出现。为了保护数据隐私、实现离线使用以及提供与本地上下文的响应式交互，在最终用户附近部署 LLM 代理的边缘越来越有必要。然而，边缘设备的严格内存限制限制了小型LLM的部署，其推理能力比大型云模型弱得多，阻碍了边缘代理AI的实际部署。测试时间缩放 (TTS) 提供了一种很有前景的解决方案，它可以在推理过程中分配更多的计算量，从而增强边缘 LLM 的推理能力。然而，当前的 TTS 方法在资源受限的设备上引入了沉重的硬件性能开销，使得它们在实际应用中不切实际。为了应对这一挑战，我们推出了 FastTTS，这是一种服务系统，可为内存受限的 LLM 推理提供快速高效的 TTS。在分析了各种 TTS 方法的常见模式并确定其性能瓶颈后，我们介绍了三种新颖的技术：i) 推测束扩展，它可以减轻由不规则推理路径引起的系统落后；ii) 非对称多模型内存分配，它可以动态平衡令牌生成和推理步骤验证之间的内存使用；以及 iii) 动态前缀感知调度，它可以优化推理执行，以最大限度地提高跨搜索路径的 KV 缓存重用。 FastTTS 在 vLLM 之上提供即插即用的第三方库，支持单个消费级 GPU (24 GB) 上的边缘 LLM (łeq 7B)，以匹配云模型的准确性和云测量的延迟。综合评估表明，与 vLLM 基线相比，FastTTS 平均吞吐量提高了 2.2 倍，延迟降低了 38%-68%；它突破了内存受限边缘设备上低延迟测试时间扩展的界限，并凸显了代理人工智能民主化的潜力。

papers.title: Finding Reusable Instructions via E-Graph Anti-Unification.
papers.abstract:
Domain-specific accelerators provide an increasingly valuable source of performance for diverse applications. Custom instructions that trigger the execution of dedicated hardware units or accelerators for common application functions become key building blocks in modern computing systems, balancing performance and cost effectiveness. RISC-V, the open and extensible instruction set architecture, is increasingly popularizing this trend. However, exploring custom instructions for an application domain remains challenging. Existing automated approaches suffer from poor reusability and limited performance. They can only identify or merge syntactically similar, scalar instruction sequences while missing semantically equivalent patterns.

We present ISAMORE, an end-to-end framework for discovering reusable custom instructions from domain applications. ISAMORE encodes general applications in an e-graph by constructing a structured domain-specific language. Its core methodology, reusable instruction identification ( RII ), leverages e-graph anti-unification (AU) to identify semantically equivalent common patterns across diverse applications, fully unleashing the potential of custom instructions. RII employs a phase-oriented iterative process with smart heuristics to enhance the scalability when dealing with real-world codebases. Besides, RII introduces the novel pattern vectorization technique, packing common operations from scalar programs into lanes of vectorized custom instructions to exploit data-level parallelism. Moreover, RII 's Pareto-optimal pattern selection balances performance gains with area overheads, guided by a profiling-based hardware-aware cost model. Evaluation demonstrates ISAMORE 's substantial performance gains, 1.12x-2.69x, over baseline approaches. We also demonstrate ISAMORE 's practical potentials using various case studies, including library analysis for three application domains and hardware specialization for quantized LLM inference and post-quantum cryptography.
papers.abstract_zh:
特定领域的加速器为不同的应用程序提供了越来越有价值的性能来源。触发执行常见应用程序功能的专用硬件单元或加速器的自定义指令成为现代计算系统中的关键构建块，平衡性能和成本效益。 RISC-V这种开放且可扩展的指令集架构正在日益普及这一趋势。然而，探索应用程序域的自定义指令仍然具有挑战性。现有的自动化方法存在可重用性差和性能有限的问题。它们只能识别或合并语法上相似的标量指令序列，而缺少语义上等效的模式。

我们提出了 ISAMORE，一个端到端框架，用于从领域应用程序中发现可重用的自定义指令。 ISAMORE 通过构建结构化的特定领域语言，对电子图中的通用应用程序进行编码。其核心方法论是可重用指令识别（RII），利用电子图反统一（AU）来识别不同应用程序中语义等效的通用模式，充分释放自定义指令的潜力。 RII 采用具有智能启发式的面向阶段的迭代过程，以增强处理实际代码库时的可扩展性。此外，RII 引入了新颖的模式向量化技术，将标量程序中的常见操作打包到向量化自定义指令通道中，以利用数据级并行性。此外，RII 的帕累托最优模式选择在基于分析的硬件感知成本模型的指导下，平衡了性能增益和面积开销。评估表明 ISAMORE 的性能比基准方法显着提高 1.12 倍至 2.69 倍。我们还通过各种案例研究展示了 ISAMORE 的实际潜力，包括三个应用领域的库分析以及量化 LLM 推理和后量子密码学的硬件专业化。

papers.title: Fine-grained and Non-intrusive LLM Training Monitoring via Microsecond-level Traffic Measurement.
papers.abstract: Large language model (LLM) training is prone to anomalies due to its long duration and large scale, which can lead to significant performance degradation or even training crashes. Due to the synchronization nature of LLM training, anomalies exhibit the cascading effect, making their diagnosis challenging. Existing approaches rely on collecting communication operator information via code instrumentation, which yields only coarse-grained monitoring data and requires modifications to training code or communication libraries. We propose Pulse, a fine-grained, non-intrusive, and easy-to-deploy monitoring system. Our key idea is to enable fine-grained monitoring via traffic measurement. Pulse conducts microsecond-level RDMA traffic measurement on NICs, and transforms flow-level measurements into communication operator measurements, thereby enabling fine-grained and non-intrusive monitoring. We deploy Pulse on a testbed with 64 H200 GPUs and evaluate its anomaly localization capability under common failure scenarios. Pulse achieves machine-level localization in 10 out of 12 scenarios, while existing methods succeed in only 4 and even misdiagnose 2 of the remaining scenarios. Additionally, Pulse achieves over 90% precision and 100% recall, supports up to 2000 concurrent RDMA flow measurements per NIC, and imposes negligible overhead on training performance, making it a practical solution for real-world LLM training environments.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）训练由于持续时间长、规模大，容易出现异常，导致性能显着下降甚至训练崩溃。由于法学硕士培训的同步性质，异常现象表现出级联效应，使其诊断具有挑战性。现有方法依赖于通过代码检测收集通信运营商信息，这仅产生粗粒度的监控数据，并且需要修改训练代码或通信库。我们建议使用 Pulse，这是一种细粒度、非侵入式且易于部署的监控系统。我们的主要想法是通过流量测量来实现细粒度的监控。 Pulse对网卡进行微秒级的RDMA流量测量，并将流量级测量转化为通信运营商测量，从而实现细粒度、非侵入式监控。我们在具有 64 个 H200 GPU 的测试台上部署 Pulse，并评估其在常见故障场景下的异常定位能力。 Pulse 在 12 个场景中的 10 个场景中实现了机器级定位，而现有方法仅在 4 个场景中成功，甚至误诊了其余场景中的 2 个。此外，Pulse 实现了超过 90% 的精度和 100% 的召回率，支持每个 NIC 多达 2000 个并发 RDMA 流量测量，并且对训练性能的开销可以忽略不计，使其成为现实 LLM 训练环境的实用解决方案。

papers.title: FlashMem: Supporting Modern DNN Workloads on Mobile with GPU Memory Hierarchy Optimizations.
papers.abstract: The increasing size and complexity of modern deep neural networks (DNNs) pose significant challenges for on-device inference on mobile GPUs, with limited memory and computational resources. Existing DNN acceleration frameworks primarily deploy a weight preloading strategy, where all model parameters are loaded into memory before execution on mobile GPUs. We posit that this approach is not adequate for modern DNN workloads that comprise very large model(s) and possibly execution of several distinct models in succession. In this work, we introduce FlashMem, a memory streaming framework designed to efficiently execute large-scale modern DNNs and multi-DNN workloads while minimizing memory consumption and reducing inference latency. Instead of fully preloading weights, FlashMem statically determines model loading schedules and dynamically streams them on demand, leveraging 2.5D texture memory to minimize data transformations and improve execution efficiency. Experimental results on 11 models demonstrate that FlashMem achieves 2.0× to 8.4× memory reduction and 1.7× to 75.0× speedup compared to existing frameworks, enabling efficient execution of large-scale models and multi-DNN support on resource-constrained mobile GPUs.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代深度神经网络 (DNN) 的规模和复杂性不断增加，对内存和计算资源有限的移动 GPU 上的设备端推理提出了重大挑战。现有的 DNN 加速框架主要部署权重预加载策略，即所有模型参数在移动 GPU 上执行之前加载到内存中。我们认为这种方法不足以满足包含非常大的模型并且可能连续执行多个不同模型的现代 DNN 工作负载。在这项工作中，我们引入了 FlashMem，这是一种内存流框架，旨在高效执行大规模现代 DNN 和多 DNN 工作负载，同时最大限度地减少内存消耗并减少推理延迟。 FlashMem 不是完全预加载权重，而是静态确定模型加载计划并按需动态流式传输，利用 2.5D 纹理内存最大限度地减少数据转换并提高执行效率。 11个模型的实验结果表明，与现有框架相比，FlashMem实现了2.0×至8.4×的内存减少和1.7×至75.0×的加速，从而能够在资源有限的移动GPU上高效执行大规模模型和多DNN支持。

papers.title: FuseFlow: A Fusion-Centric Compilation Framework for Sparse Deep Learning on Streaming Dataflow.
papers.abstract: As deep learning models scale, sparse deep learning (DL) models that exploit sparsity in weights, activations, or inputs and specialized dataflow hardware have emerged as powerful solutions to address efficiency. We propose FuseFlow, a compiler that converts sparse machine learning models written in PyTorch to fused sparse dataflow graphs for reconfigurable dataflow architectures (RDAs). FuseFlow is the first compiler to support general cross-expression fusion of sparse operations. In addition to fusion across kernels (expressions), FuseFlow also supports optimizations like parallelization, dataflow ordering, and sparsity blocking. It targets a cycle-accurate dataflow simulator for microarchitectural analysis of fusion strategies. We use FuseFlow for design-space exploration across four real-world machine learning applications with sparsity, showing that full fusion (entire cross-expression fusion across all computation in an end-to-end model) is not always optimal for sparse models—fusion granularity depends on the model itself. FuseFlow also provides a heuristic to identify and prune suboptimal configurations. Using FuseFlow, we achieve performance improvements, including a ~2.7x speedup over an unfused baseline for GPT-3 with BigBird block-sparse attention.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着深度学习模型的扩展，利用权重、激活或输入的稀疏性和专用数据流硬件的稀疏深度学习 (DL) 模型已成为解决效率问题的强大解决方案。我们提出了 FuseFlow，一种编译器，它将用 PyTorch 编写的稀疏机器学习模型转换为融合稀疏数据流图，以实现可重新配置的数据流架构 (RDA)。 FuseFlow 是第一个支持稀疏操作的通用交叉表达式融合的编译器。除了跨内核（表达式）融合之外，FuseFlow 还支持并行化、数据流排序和稀疏阻塞等优化。它的目标是一个周期精确的数据流模拟器，用于融合策略的微架构分析。我们使用 FuseFlow 在四个稀疏的现实机器学习应用程序中进行设计空间探索，表明完全融合（端到端模型中所有计算的整个交叉表达式融合）对于稀疏模型并不总是最佳的 - 融合粒度取决于模型本身。 FuseFlow 还提供启发式方法来识别和修剪次优配置。使用 FuseFlow，我们实现了性能改进，包括与具有 BigBird 块稀疏注意力的 GPT-3 的未融合基线相比，速度提高了约 2.7 倍。

papers.title: GFS: A Preemption-aware Scheduling Framework for GPU Clusters with Predictive Spot Instance Management.
papers.abstract: The surge in large language models (LLMs) has fundamentally reshaped the landscape of GPU usage patterns, creating an urgent need for more efficient management strategies. While cloud providers employ spot instances to reduce costs for low-priority (LP) tasks, existing schedulers still grapple with high eviction rates and lengthy queuing times. To address these limitations, we present GFS, a novel preemptive scheduling framework that enhances service-level objective (SLO) compliance for high-priority (HP) tasks while minimizing preemptions to LP tasks. Firstly, GFS utilizes a lightweight forecasting model that predicts GPU demand among different tenants, enabling proactive resource management. Secondly, GFS employs a dynamic allocation mechanism to adjust the spot quota for LP tasks with guaranteed durations. Lastly, GFS incorporates a preemptive scheduling policy that prioritizes HP tasks while minimizing the impact on LP tasks. We demonstrate the effectiveness of GFS through both real-world implementation and simulations. The results show that GFS reduces eviction rates by 33.0%, and cuts queuing delays by 44.1% for LP tasks. Furthermore, GFS enhances the GPU allocation rate by up to 22.8% in real production clusters. In a production cluster of more than 10,000 GPUs, GFS yields roughly $459,715 in monthly benefits.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 的激增从根本上重塑了 GPU 使用模式的格局，迫切需要更高效的管理策略。虽然云提供商采用现货实例来降低低优先级 (LP) 任务的成本，但现有的调度程序仍然面临着高驱逐率和漫长的排队时间的问题。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了 GFS，这是一种新颖的抢占式调度框架，可增强高优先级 (HP) 任务的服务级别目标 (SLO) 合规性，同时最大限度地减少对 LP 任务的抢占。首先，GFS利用轻量级预测模型来预测不同租户之间的GPU需求，从而实现主动的资源管理。其次，GFS采用动态分配机制来调整保证工期的LP任务的现货配额。最后，GFS 结合了抢占式调度策略，可以优先考虑 HP 任务，同时最大限度地减少对 LP 任务的影响。我们通过现实世界的实施和模拟证明了 GFS 的有效性。结果表明，GFS 将 LP 任务的驱逐率降低了 33.0%，并将排队延迟减少了 44.1%。此外，GFS在实际生产集群中将GPU分配率提高了高达22.8%。在超过 10,000 个 GPU 的生产集群中，GFS 每月产生大约 459,715 美元的收益。

papers.title: Graphiti: Formally Verified Out-of-Order Execution in Dataflow Circuits.
papers.abstract:
High-level synthesis (HLS) tools automatically synthesise hardware from imperative programs and have seen a significant rise in adoption in both industry and academia. To deliver high-quality hardware designs for increasingly general purpose programs, HLS compilers have to become more aggressive. For the most irregular programs, HLS tools generating dataflow circuits show promising performance by adapting and specializing key ideas from processor architectures, like out-of-order execution and speculation. However, the complexity of these transformations makes them difficult to reason about, increasing the risk of subtle bugs and potentially delaying their adoption in a conservative industry where bugs can be extremely costly.

This paper introduces G raphiti , a framework embedded in the Lean 4 proof assistant designed to formally reason about and manipulate dataflow circuits at the core of these HLS tools. We develop a metatheory of graph refinement that allows us to verify a general-purpose dataflow circuit rewriting algorithm. Using this framework, we formally verify a loop rewrite that introduces out-of-order execution into a dataflow circuit. Our evaluation shows that the resulting verified optimization pipeline achieves a 2.1× speedup over the in-order HLS flow and a 5.8× speedup over a verified HLS tool generating a static state machine. We also show that it achieves the same performance compared to an existing unverified approach which introduces out-of-order execution.

G raphiti is a step toward a fully-verified HLS flow targeting dataflow circuits. In the interim, it can serve as an extensible, verified, optimizing engine that can be integrated into existing dataflow HLS compilers.
papers.abstract_zh:
高级综合 (HLS) 工具自动从命令式程序综合硬件，并且在工业界和学术界的采用率显着上升。为了为日益通用的程序提供高质量的硬件设计，HLS 编译器必须变得更加积极。对于最不规则的程序，生成数据流电路的 HLS 工具通过调整和专门化处理器架构中的关键思想（例如乱序执行和推测）而显示出良好的性能。然而，这些转换的复杂性使得它们难以推理，增加了出现细微错误的风险，并可能延迟它们在保守行业中的采用，因为错误的成本可能极高。

本文介绍了 G raphiti，这是一个嵌入 Lean 4 证明助手中的框架，旨在形式化推理和操作这些 HLS 工具核心的数据流电路。我们开发了一种图细化的元理论，使我们能够验证通用数据流电路重写算法。使用这个框架，我们正式验证了将无序执行引入数据流电路的循环重写。我们的评估表明，所得到的经过验证的优化管道比有序 HLS 流实现了 2.1 倍的加速，比生成静态状态机的经过验证的 HLS 工具实现了 5.8 倍的加速。我们还表明，与引入乱序执行的现有未经验证的方法相比，它实现了相同的性能。

G raphiti 是朝着针对数据流电路的经过完全验证的 HLS 流迈出的一步。在此期间，它可以充当可扩展、经过验证的优化引擎，可以集成到现有的数据流 HLS 编译器中。

papers.title: GS-Scale: Unlocking Large-Scale 3D Gaussian Splatting Training via Host Offloading.
papers.abstract: The advent of 3D Gaussian Splatting has revolutionized graphics rendering by delivering high visual quality and fast rendering speeds. However, training large-scale scenes at high quality remains challenging due to the substantial memory demands required to store parameters, gradients, and optimizer states, which can quickly overwhelm GPU memory. To address these limitations, we propose GS-Scale, a fast and memory-efficient training system for 3D Gaussian Splatting. GS-Scale stores all Gaussians in host memory, transferring only a subset to the GPU on demand for each forward and backward pass. While this dramatically reduces GPU memory usage, it requires frustum culling and optimizer updates to be executed on the CPU, introducing slowdowns due to CPU's limited compute and memory bandwidth. To mitigate this, GS-Scale employs three system-level optimizations: (1) selective offloading of geometric parameters for fast frustum culling, (2) parameter forwarding to pipeline CPU optimizer updates with GPU computation, and (3) deferred optimizer update to minimize unnecessary memory accesses for Gaussians with zero gradients. Our extensive evaluations on large-scale datasets demonstrate that GS-Scale significantly lowers GPU memory demands by 3.3-5.6x, while achieving training speeds comparable to GPU without host offloading. This enables large-scale 3D Gaussian Splatting training on consumer-grade GPUs; for instance, GS-Scale can scale the number of Gaussians from 4 million to 18 million on an RTX 4070 Mobile GPU, leading to 23-35% LPIPS (learned perceptual image patch similarity) improvement.
papers.abstract_zh: 3D 高斯溅射的出现通过提供高视觉质量和快速渲染速度彻底改变了图形渲染。然而，由于存储参数、梯度和优化器状态需要大量内存，因此高质量训练大规模场景仍然具有挑战性，这可能会很快耗尽 GPU 内存。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了 GS-Scale，这是一种快速且内存高效的 3D 高斯分布训练系统。 GS-Scale 将所有高斯分布存储在主机内存中，每次前向和后向传递时仅根据需要将一个子集传输到 GPU。虽然这极大地减少了 GPU 内存使用量，但它需要在 CPU 上执行视锥体剔除和优化器更新，从而由于 CPU 的计算和内存带宽有限而导致速度减慢。为了缓解这一问题，GS-Scale 采用了三种系统级优化：(1) 选择性卸载几何参数以实现快速视锥剔除，(2) 通过 GPU 计算将参数转发到管道 CPU 优化器更新，以及 (3) 延迟优化器更新以最大程度地减少零梯度高斯函数的不必要的内存访问。我们对大规模数据集的广泛评估表明，GS-Scale 将 GPU 内存需求显着降低了 3.3-5.6 倍，同时在没有主机卸载的情况下实现了与 GPU 相当的训练速度。这使得能够在消费级 GPU 上进行大规模 3D 高斯泼溅训练；例如，GS-Scale 可以在 RTX 4070 移动 GPU 上将高斯数量从 400 万扩展到 1800 万，从而将 LPIPS（学习感知图像块相似度）提高 23-35%。

papers.title: gShare: Efficient GPU Sharing with Aggressive Scheduling in Multi-tenant FaaS platform.
papers.abstract: Serving ML models with serverless computing has become increasingly popular in recent years. Many of today's cloud vendors have provided GPU functions to meet the performance requirements of different ML scenarios. However, existing production FaaS platforms suffer from GPU under-utilization and high cloud costs due to poor GPU resource management. In this paper, we propose gShare, an on-demand and efficient GPU function management policy in FaaS platforms. gShare provides a fine-grained GPU virtualization solution for a VM-based multi-tenant FaaS environment. It further decouples the GPU resource from the existing CPU-oriented function management paradigm, enabling flexible GPU sharing across tenants. With a user-transparent vGPU remapping design and aggressive request scheduling policy, gShare can significantly improve the cost-efficiency of GPU functions without causing appreciable function performance degradation. Experimental results show that gShare can reduce GPU usage by 43%–63% compared to the baseline while meeting more than 95% of user latency targets. Compared with the state-of-the-art method, it can also reduce cloud costs by 24%-58% while maintaining better function performance, benefiting both the cloud provider and users.
papers.abstract_zh: 近年来，通过无服务器计算提供机器学习模型变得越来越流行。当今许多云供应商都提供了GPU功能来满足不同ML场景的性能要求。然而，现有的生产 FaaS 平台因 GPU 资源管理不善而面临 GPU 利用率不足和云成本高昂的问题。在本文中，我们提出了 gShare，一种 FaaS 平台中按需且高效的 GPU 功能管理策略。 gShare为基于VM的多租户FaaS环境提供了细粒度的GPU虚拟化解决方案。它将GPU资源与现有的面向CPU的功能管理范式进一步解耦，实现跨租户灵活的GPU共享。凭借用户透明的vGPU重映射设计和积极的请求调度策略，gShare可以显着提高GPU功能的成本效率，而不会导致明显的功能性能下降。实验结果表明，与基线相比，gShare 可以减少 GPU 使用率 43%–63%，同时满足 95% 以上的用户延迟目标。与最先进的方法相比，它还可以在保持更好的功能性能的同时降低云成本24%-58%，使云提供商和用户都受益。

papers.title: Hardwired-Neuron Language Processing Units as General-Purpose Cognitive Substrates.
papers.abstract:
The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has established language as a core general-purpose cognitive substrate, driving the demand for specialized Language Processing Units (LPUs) tailored for LLM inference. To overcome the growing energy consumption of LLM inference systems, this paper proposes a Hardwired-Neurons Language Processing Unit (HNLPU), which physically hardwires LLM weight parameters into the computational fabric, achieving several orders of magnitude computational efficiency improvement by extreme specialization. However, a significant challenge still lies in the scale of modern LLMs. A straightforward hardwiring of GPT-OSS-120B would require fabricating photomask sets valued at over 6 billion dollars, rendering this straightforward solution economically impractical.

Addressing this challenge, we propose the novel Metal-Embedding methodology. Instead of embedding weights in a 2D grid of silicon device cells, Metal-Embedding embeds weight parameters into the 3D topology of metal wires. This brings two benefits: (1) a 15× increase in density, and (2) 60 out of 70 photomask layers are homogeneous across chips, including all EUV photomasks. In total, Metal-Embedding reduced the photomask cost by 112×, bringing the Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) cost of HNLPU into an economically viable range. Experimental results show that HNLPU achieved 249,960 tokens/s (5,555×/85× that of GPU/WSE), 36 tokens/J (1,047×/283× that of GPU/WSE), 13,232 mm2 total die area, 59.46 M-123.5 M estimated NRE at 5 nm technology. Analysis shows that HNLPU achieved 41.7-80.4× improvement in cost-effectiveness and 357× reduction in carbon footprint compared to OpenAI-scale H100 clusters, under an annual weight updating assumption.
papers.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型 (LLM) 的快速发展已将语言确立为核心通用认知基础，推动了对专为 LLM 推理量身定制的专用语言处理单元 (LPU) 的需求。为了克服LLM推理系统不断增长的能耗，本文提出了一种硬连线神经元语言处理单元（HNLPU），它将LLM权重参数物理硬连线到计算结构中，通过极端专业化实现了几个数量级的计算效率提高。然而，现代法学硕士的规模仍然是一个重大挑战。 GPT-OSS-120B 的直接硬连线需要制造价值超过 60 亿美元的光掩模组，使得这种简单的解决方案在经济上不切实际。

为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了新颖的金属嵌入方法。金属嵌入不是将权重嵌入到硅器件单元的 2D 网格中，而是将权重参数嵌入到金属线的 3D 拓扑中。这带来了两个好处：(1) 密度增加了 15 倍，(2) 70 个光掩模层中有 60 个在芯片上是同质的，包括所有 EUV 光掩模。总的来说，金属嵌入将光掩模成本降低了 112 倍，使 HNLPU 的一次性工程 (NRE) 成本达到经济可行的范围。实验结果表明，HNLPU 在 5 nm 技术下实现了 249,960 个 token/s（GPU/WSE 的 5,555×/85×）、36 个 token/J（GPU/WSE 的 1,047×/283×）、13,232 mm2 总芯片面积、59.46 M-123.5 M 估计 NRE。分析表明，在每年权重更新的假设下，与 OpenAI 规模的 H100 集群相比，HNLPU 的成本效益提高了 41.7-80.4 倍，碳足迹减少了 357 倍。

papers.title: HEPIC: Private Inference over Homomorphic Encryption with Client Intervention.
papers.abstract: Homomorphic Encryption (HE) enables Private Inference (PI) in Machine Learning as a Service (MLaaS), protecting both client inputs and server-side neural network (NN) parameters. Existing PI techniques are predominantly implemented as either HE-based fire-and-forget methods or MPC-based interactive methods. Recent HE-based PI systems improve the accuracy--performance trade-off via a layer-wise scheme and parameter switching, yet remain bottlenecked by fire-and-forget execution in which the server alone performs costly ciphertext management (e.g., bootstrapping and scheme/parameter conversions). We present HEPIC, an HE-based PI system that explores a different design point by leveraging client interventions for ciphertext managements. In a sense, HEPIC shares a common ground with MPC-based PI of being interactive with the client, but differs in that the client only intervenes for ciphertext managements required in HE operations. Because ciphertext management has identical semantics on the client and the server, HEPIC lets developers decide where and how often to execute it, enabling fine-grained trade-offs among computation, communication, and ciphertext configuration. HEPIC makes such execution practical by overlapping client re-encryption, server computation, and communication via dependency-aware pipelining and streaming-based transfers. We further enhance the performance with a cache-aware task allocator (CATA) and a cost-aware client intervention scheduler (CACIS) to exploit ciphertext-level parallelism and to mitigate stalls under client-server performance disparity. Our evaluation shows that HEPIC achieves up to 2.20--41.93× speedup over state-of-the-art fire-and-forget HE-based PI, while maintaining zero loss in inference accuracy.
papers.abstract_zh: 同态加密 (HE) 支持机器学习即服务 (MLaaS) 中的私有推理 (PI)，保护客户端输入和服务器端神经网络 (NN) 参数。现有的 PI 技术主要以基于 HE 的即发即弃方法或基于 MPC 的交互式方法来实现。最近基于 HE 的 PI 系统通过分层方案和参数切换提高了准确性和性能权衡，但仍然受到“即发即忘”执行的瓶颈，其中服务器单独执行昂贵的密文管理（例如，引导和方案/参数转换）。我们推出 HEPIC，这是一个基于 HE 的 PI 系统，它通过利用客户干预进行密文管理来探索不同的设计点。从某种意义上说，HEPIC 与基于 MPC 的 PI 的共同点是与客户端交互，但不同之处在于客户端仅干预 HE 操作所需的密文管理。由于密文管理在客户端和服务器上具有相同的语义，HEPIC 允许开发人员决定执行位置和执行频率，从而实现计算、通信和密文配置之间的细粒度权衡。 HEPIC 通过重叠客户端重新加密、服务器计算以及通过依赖性感知管道和基于流的传输进行通信，使此类执行变得实用。我们通过缓存感知任务分配器（CATA）和成本感知客户端干预调度程序（CACIS）进一步增强性能，以利用密文级并行性并减轻客户端-服务器性能差异下的停顿。我们的评估表明，与最先进的基于 HE 的 PI 相比，HEPIC 实现了高达 2.20--41.93 倍的加速，同时保持推理精度零损失。

papers.title: Highly Automated Verification of Security Properties for Unmodified System Software.
papers.abstract: System software is often complex and hides exploitable security vulnerabilities. Formal verification promises bug-free software but comes with a prohibitive proof cost. We present Spoq2, the first verification framework to highly automate security verification of unmodified system software. Spoq2 is based on the observation that many security properties, such as noninterference, can be reduced to establishing inductive invariants on individual transitions of a transition system that models system software. However, directly verifying such invariants for real system code overwhelms existing SMT solvers. Spoq2 makes this possible by automatically reducing verification complexity. It decomposes transitions into individual execution paths, extends cone-of-influence analysis to the individual transition level, and eliminates irrelevant machine states, clauses, and control-flow paths before invoking the SMT solver. Spoq2 further optimizes how pointer operations are modeled and verified through pointer abstractions that eliminate expensive bit-wise operations from SMT queries. We demonstrate the effectiveness of Spoq2 by verifying security properties of four unmodified, real-world system codebases with minimal manual effort.
papers.abstract_zh: 系统软件通常很复杂并且隐藏着可利用的安全漏洞。形式验证承诺软件不会出现错误，但其证明成本却令人望而却步。我们推出了 Spoq2，这是第一个对未修改的系统软件进行高度自动化安全验证的验证框架。 Spoq2 基于这样的观察：许多安全属性（例如不干扰）可以简化为在对系统软件进行建模的转换系统的各个转换上建立归纳不变量。然而，直接验证真实系统代码的此类不变量会压垮现有的 SMT 求解器。 Spoq2 通过自动降低验证复杂性使这成为可能。它将转换分解为单独的执行路径，将影响​​锥分析扩展到单独的转换级别，并在调用 SMT 求解器之前消除不相关的机器状态、子句和控制流路径。 Spoq2 通过指针抽象进一步优化了指针操作的建模和验证方式，消除了 SMT 查询中昂贵的按位操作。我们通过以最少的手动操作验证四个未经修改的真实系统代码库的安全属性来证明 Spoq2 的有效性。

papers.title: History Doesn't Repeat Itself but Rollouts Rhyme: Accelerating Reinforcement Learning with RhymeRL.
papers.abstract: With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a pivotal methodology for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of LLMs. Unlike traditional pre-training approaches, RL encompasses multiple stages: rollout, reward, and training, which necessitates collaboration among various worker types. However, current RL systems continue to grapple with substantial GPU underutilization, due to two primary factors: (1) The rollout stage dominates the overall RL process due to test-time scaling; (2) Imbalances in rollout lengths (within the same batch) result in GPU bubbles. While prior solutions like asynchronous execution and truncation offer partial relief, they may compromise training accuracy for efficiency. Our key insight stems from a previously overlooked observation: rollout responses exhibit remarkable similarity across adjacent training epochs. Based on the insight, we introduce RhymeRL, an LLM RL system designed to accelerate RL training with two key innovations. First, to enhance rollout generation, we present HistoSpec, a speculative decoding inference engine that utilizes the similarity of historical rollout token sequences to obtain accurate drafts. Second, to tackle rollout bubbles, we introduce HistoPipe, a two-tier scheduling strategy that leverages the similarity of historical rollout distributions to balance workload among rollout workers. Experimental results demonstrate that RhymeRL achieves up to a 2.6x performance improvement over existing methods, without compromising accuracy or modifying the RL paradigm.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLM）的快速发展，强化学习（RL）已成为增强LLM推理能力的关键方法。与传统的预训练方法不同，强化学习包含多个阶段：推出、奖励和训练，这需要不同类型的工作人员之间进行协作。然而，由于两个主要因素，当前的 RL 系统继续面临 GPU 利用率不足的问题：(1) 由于测试时间的扩展，推出阶段主导了整个 RL 过程； (2) rollout长度不平衡（同一批次内）导致GPU气泡。虽然异步执行和截断等先前的解决方案提供了部分缓解，但它们可能会损害训练的准确性以提高效率。我们的关键见解源于之前被忽视的观察：推出反应在相邻的训练时期表现出显着的相似性。基于这一见解，我们推出了 RhymeRL，这是一种 LLM RL 系统，旨在通过两项关键创新来加速 RL 训练。首先，为了增强 rollout 生成，我们提出了 HistoSpec，这是一种推测性解码推理引擎，它利用历史 rollout 令牌序列的相似性来获得准确的草稿。其次，为了解决推出泡沫问题，我们引入了 HistoPipe，这是一种两层调度策略，利用历史推出分布的相似性来平衡推出工作人员之间的工作量。实验结果表明，在不影响准确性或修改 RL 范式的情况下，RhymeRL 的性能比现有方法提高了 2.6 倍。

papers.title: Hitchhike: Efficient Request Submission via Deferred Enforcement of Address Contiguity.
papers.abstract:
Modern storage systems operate under high concurrency, making large volumes of outstanding I/Os the norm. However, current I/O submission logic requires requests assigned to the same CPU core to be processed in a serialized manner, turning the software stack into a bottleneck due to high per-request overhead.

We argue that the primary cause of this inefficiency lies in the end-to-end enforcement of the strict address contiguity validation -- the constraint that each I/O request must access a contiguous range of addresses (e.g., file offsets, sectors, and logical block addresses). Through system-level analysis, we find that while the address contiguity requirement is crucial at the device level as defined by the NVMe protocol, it is unnecessarily enforced throughout the entire I/O stack. Based on this insight, we propose Hitchhike, an efficient request submission logic that defers address contiguity validation to the device driver. Unlike solutions that resort to kernel-bypass or aggressive polling for performance, Hitchhike achieves high efficiency within the standard OS stack by allowing a single request to encapsulate multiple non-contiguous address ranges. This mechanism drastically reduces the number of individual requests traversing the stack, thereby amortizing the kernel's per-request overhead.

We apply Hitchhike to a graph engine, a B-tree store, and FIO. Experimental results show that Hitchhike effectively improves throughput and reduces CPU overhead, while preserving compatibility with existing kernel semantics and hardware interfaces.
papers.abstract_zh:
现代存储系统在高并发下运行，大量未处理的 I/O 成为常态。然而，当前的 I/O 提交逻辑需要以串行方式处理分配给同一 CPU 核心的请求，由于每个请求的开销较高，因此将软件堆栈变成了瓶颈。

我们认为，这种低效率的主要原因在于严格地址连续性验证的端到端执行——每个 I/O 请求必须访问连续地址范围（例如文件偏移、扇区和逻辑块地址）的约束。通过系统级分析，我们发现虽然地址连续性要求在 NVMe 协议定义的设备级至关重要，但它在整个 I/O 堆栈中没有必要强制执行。基于这一见解，我们提出了 Hitchhike，一种高效的请求提交逻辑，它将地址连续性验证推迟到设备驱动程序。与采用内核绕过或积极轮询来提高性能的解决方案不同，Hitchhike 通过允许单个请求封装多个不连续的地址范围，在标准操作系统堆栈中实现了高效率。这种机制大大减少了遍历堆栈的单个请求的数量，从而分摊了内核的每个请求的开销。

我们将 Hitchhike 应用于图形引擎、B 树存储和 FIO。实验结果表明，Hitchhike 有效提高了吞吐量并降低了 CPU 开销，同时保持了与现有内核语义和硬件接口的兼容性。

papers.title: I/O Analysis is All You Need: An I/O Analysis for Long-Sequence Attention.
papers.abstract: As GPUs and other accelerators become increasingly popular, optimizing I/O operations between on-chip and off-chip memory is increasingly critical. I/O analysis, however, is complex, requiring a deep understanding of application dataflow and memory hierarchy. Developing a practical I/O analysis methodology remains a timely challenge. Self-attention is employed extensively in transformer models, but its quadratic memory complexity poses significant challenges to modern memory systems. In this study, we explore how to use I/O analysis to develop optimal solutions for accelerating exact long-sequence self-attention. We first introduce a novel I/O analysis for tall-and-skinny matrix-matrix multiplication, which captures the dominant data movement behavior of long-sequence self-attention. Guided by systematic I/O analysis, we develop AttenIO, an I/O-driven accelerator for exact long-sequence self-attention with three key optimizations: (1) an analytically derived I/O-optimal tiling and scheduling to minimize I/O operations, (2) fine-grained three-level communication-computation overlapping to hide I/O stalls, and (3) parallel execution patterns for efficient softmax. Our evaluation shows that AttenIO achieves a 1.6×-8.8× speedup over the state-of-the-art solutions. Although AttenIO is designed for self-attention, it also highlights the broader potential of I/O analysis as a principled foundation for guiding high-performance I/O optimizations.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着 GPU 和其他加速器变得越来越流行，优化片上和片外存储器之间的 I/O 操作变得越来越重要。然而，I/O 分析很复杂，需要深入了解应用程序数据流和内存层次结构。开发实用的 I/O 分析方法仍然是一项及时的挑战。自注意力在 Transformer 模型中得到广泛应用，但其二次内存复杂性给现代内存系统带来了重大挑战。在本研究中，我们探索如何使用 I/O 分析来开发加速精确长序列自注意力的最佳解决方案。我们首先介绍一种新颖的高瘦矩阵-矩阵乘法 I/O 分析，它捕获了长序列自注意力的主要数据移动行为。在系统 I/O 分析的指导下，我们开发了 AttenIO，一种 I/O 驱动的加速器，用于精确的长序列自注意力，具有三个关键优化：(1) 分析得出的 I/O 最佳平铺和调度，以最大限度地减少 I/O 操作，(2) 细粒度的三级通信计算重叠，以隐藏 I/O 停顿，以及 (3) 高效 softmax 的并行执行模式。我们的评估表明，AttenIO 比最先进的解决方案实现了 1.6×-8.8× 的加速。尽管 AttenIO 是为自注意力而设计的，但它也强调了 I/O 分析作为指导高性能 I/O 优化的原则基础的更广泛潜力。

papers.title: ICARUS: Criticality and Reuse based Instruction Caching for Datacenter Applications.
papers.abstract:
Datacenter applications with huge code footprints suffer from front-end CPU bottlenecks even with a decoupled front-end. These applications are composed of complex system stacks with subtle interdependencies. One of the primary contributors to the front-end bottleneck is instruction misses at L2, which cause decode starvation. State-of-the-art L2 cache replacement policies, such as EMISSARY, utilize front-end criticality to identify instruction lines that cause decode starvation and attempt to keep those critical lines in L2. We observe that only 28.32% of the critical lines retain their criticality behavior, and a significant fraction of the critical instruction lines show dynamic behavior.

We propose ICARUS, an L2 replacement policy that incorporates branch history as context information to improve critical instruction line detection. We observe that the reuse distance of instruction lines varies based on the branch history that led to the instruction fetch. Next, we enhance the L2 replacement policy by considering both criticality and the reuse of instruction lines at L2, as we observe that the reuse behavior of critical lines differs from that of non-critical lines. On average, across 12 datacenter applications, ICARUS outperforms Tree-based Pseudo LRU (TPLRU) by 5.6% and as high as 51%. The state-of-the-art replacement policy, EMISSARY, on the other hand, provides an improvement of 2.2% over TPLRU. We demonstrate the robustness of ICARUS across various L1I and L2 cache sizes, as well as its effectiveness in the presence of hardware prefetchers.
papers.abstract_zh:
即使具有解耦的前端，具有大量代码占用空间的数据中心应用程序也会遭受前端 CPU 瓶颈的困扰。这些应用程序由具有微妙相互依赖性的复杂系统堆栈组成。造成前端瓶颈的主要原因之一是 L2 处的指令缺失，这会导致解码匮乏。最先进的 L2 缓存替换策略（例如 EMISSARY）利用前端关键性来识别导致解码匮乏的指令行，并尝试将这些关键行保留在 L2 中。我们观察到，只有 28.32% 的关键线保留了其关键行为，并且很大一部分关键指令线显示出动态行为。

我们提出了 ICARUS，一种 L2 替换策略，它将分支历史记录作为上下文信息，以改进关键指令行检测。我们观察到指令行的重用距离根据导致指令获取的分支历史而变化。接下来，我们通过考虑 L2 指令行的关键性和重用来增强 L2 替换策略，因为我们观察到关键行的重用行为与非关键行的重用行为不同。平均而言，在 12 个数据中心应用程序中，ICARUS 的性能优于基于树的伪 LRU (TPLRU) 5.6%，最高可达 51%。另一方面，最先进的更换策略 EMISSARY 比 TPLRU 提高了 2.2%。我们展示了 ICARUS 在各种 L1I 和 L2 缓存大小上的稳健性，以及它在存在硬件预取器的情况下的有效性。

papers.title: Insum: Sparse GPU Kernels Simplified and Optimized with Indirect Einsums.
papers.abstract: Programming high-performance sparse GPU kernels is notoriously difficult, requiring both substantial effort and deep expertise. Sparse compilers aim to simplify this process, but existing systems fall short in two key ways. First, they are primarily designed for CPUs and rarely produce high-performance GPU code. Second, when computations involve both sparse and dense regions, these compilers often fail to optimize the dense portions effectively. In this paper, we propose a new approach for expressing sparse computations. We start from format-agnostic Einsums over sparse tensors and rewrite them into format-conscious indirect Einsums, which explicitly encode format information by mapping sparse data and metadata onto dense tensor operations through indirect indexing. To execute indirect Einsums, we introduce the Insum compiler, which generates efficient GPU code for these Einsums by lowering to the PyTorch compiler, extended to better support Tensor Core–enabled indirect Einsums. We also present two fixed-length sparse formats, GroupCOO and BlockGroupCOO, designed to fit naturally with indirect Einsums. Our approach achieves 1.14×–3.81× speedups across a range of sparse GPU applications while reducing lines of code by 202×–4491× compared to hand-written implementations. The source code for Insum is publicly available at https://github.com/nullplay/IndirectEinsum.
papers.abstract_zh: 众所周知，对高性能稀疏 GPU 内核进行编程非常困难，需要大量的努力和深厚的专业知识。稀疏编译器旨在简化此过程，但现有系统在两个关键方面存在不足。首先，它们主要是为 CPU 设计的，很少生成高性能 GPU 代码。其次，当计算同时涉及稀疏区域和密集区域时，这些编译器通常无法有效优化密集部分。在本文中，我们提出了一种表达稀疏计算的新方法。我们从稀疏张量上与格式无关的 Einsum 开始，并将它们重写为格式意识的间接 Einsum，它通过间接索引将稀疏数据和元数据映射到密集张量操作来显式编码格式信息。为了执行间接 Einsum，我们引入了 Insum 编译器，它通过降低到 PyTorch 编译器为这些 Einsum 生成高效的 GPU 代码，并进行扩展以更好地支持支持 Tensor Core 的间接 Einsum。我们还提出了两种固定长度的稀疏格式：GroupCOO 和 BlockGroupCOO，旨在自然地适应间接 Einsum。与手写实现相比，我们的方法在一系列稀疏 GPU 应用程序中实现了 1.14×–3.81× 的加速，同时减少了 202×–4491× 的代码行。 Insum 的源代码可在 https://github.com/nullplay/IndirectEinsum 上公开获取。

papers.title: iSwitch: QEC on Demand via In-Situ Encoding of Bare Qubits for Ion Trap Architectures.
papers.abstract: Recent advances in quantum hardware and error correction have paved the way for early fault-tolerant (EFT) quantum computing. We propose iSwitch, a hybrid system architecture for trapped-ion quantum computers (TIQC) that exploits ultra-high-fidelity single-qubit gates and efficient logical CNOTs enabled by ion shuttling. iSwitch employs bare qubits for single-qubit operations and QEC-encoded logical qubits for two-qubit gates, avoiding full logical encoding, gate synthesis, and magic state distillation. To enable this selective encoding, we develop a low-noise conversion protocol between bare and logical qubits, a hybrid instruction set tailored to 2D TIQC layouts, and a compiler that minimizes conversion overhead and optimizes scheduling. Evaluations on variational quantum algorithm benchmarks show that iSwitch achieves comparable fidelity to conventional QEC methods, while reducing qubit and operation counts by roughly 33–50%, offering a practical, resource-efficient path toward EFT quantum computing on trapped-ion platforms.
papers.abstract_zh: 量子硬件和纠错方面的最新进展为早期容错（EFT）量子计算铺平了道路。我们提出了 iSwitch，这是一种用于捕获离子量子计算机 (TIQC) 的混合系统架构，它利用超高保真度单量子位门和由离子穿梭实现的高效逻辑 CNOT。 iSwitch 使用裸量子位进行单量子位操作，使用 QEC 编码的逻辑量子位进行双量子位门，从而避免了完整的逻辑编码、门合成和魔态蒸馏。为了实现这种选择性编码，我们开发了裸量子位和逻辑量子位之间的低噪声转换协议、专为 2D TIQC 布局量身定制的混合指令集以及可最大限度减少转换开销并优化调度的编译器。对变分量子算法基准的评估表明，iSwitch 实现了与传统 QEC 方法相当的保真度，同时将量子位和操作数量减少了大约 33-50%，为捕获离子平台上的 EFT 量子计算提供了一条实用、资源高效的路径。

papers.title: It Takes Two to Entangle.
papers.abstract: Distributed machine learning training and inference is common today because today's large models require more memory and compute than can be provided by a single GPU. Distributed models are generally produced by programmers who take a sequential model specification and apply several distribution strategies to distribute state and computation across GPUs. Unfortunately, bugs can be introduced in the process, and a distributed model implementation's outputs might differ from the sequential model's outputs. In this paper, we describe an approach to statically identify such bugs by checking model refinement, that is, can the sequential model's outputs be reconstructed from the distributed model's outputs? Our approach, implemented in Entangle, uses iterative rewriting to prove model refinement. Our approach can scale to today's large models and deployments: we evaluate it using GPT and Llama-3. Further, it provides actionable outputs that aids in bug localization.
papers.abstract_zh: 分布式机器学习训练和推理如今很常见，因为当今的大型模型需要比单个 GPU 所能提供的更多的内存和计算量。分布式模型通常由程序员生成，他们采用顺序模型规范并应用多种分布策略来跨 GPU 分布状态和计算。不幸的是，这个过程中可能会引入错误，并且分布式模型实现的输出可能与顺序模型的输出不同。在本文中，我们描述了一种通过检查模型细化来静态识别此类错误的方法，即顺序模型的输出是否可以从分布式模型的输出中重建？我们的方法在 Entangle 中实现，使用迭代重写来证明模型的细化。我们的方法可以扩展到当今的大型模型和部署：我们使用 GPT 和 Llama-3 对其进行评估。此外，它还提供可操作的输出，有助于错误定位。

papers.title: JOSer: Just-In-Time Object Serialization for Heavy Java Serialization Workloads.
papers.abstract: Object serialization is critical in Java, which preserves objects in memory and transfers them among software systems if needed. However, the serialization techniques of modern Java systems are usually inflexible and inefficient under heavy serialization workloads, as they rely on manually-defined object schemas or omni-functional serializers. To tackle the above problem, we reveal a novel, serialization-specific optimization opportunity in Java. Based on it, we develop JOSer ( Just-in-time Object SERializer ), an efficient, Just-in-Time (JIT) object serialization technique. At runtime, JOSer generates a set of class-specific, JIT-friendly object serializers ( i.e., serialization code), and then continuously optimizes them with the JIT compiler of Java Virtual Machine (JVM). JOSer also shares the metadata of objects under serialization and the serializers under optimization. We evaluate JOSer against six Java serialization techniques including OpenJDK's built-in serialization technique. Overall, JOSer improves the throughput by up to 20~83× in serialization and 43~229× in deserialization. JOSer has been successfully deployed in real-world products, reducing serialization CPU usage of Flink by 35.32~41.14% and latency of search recommendations by 30+ ms. JOSer grounds Apache Fory#8482;, an open-source serialization framework available at https://fory.apache.org/.
papers.abstract_zh: 对象序列化在 Java 中至关重要，它将对象保存在内存中，并在需要时在软件系统之间传输它们。然而，现代 Java 系统的序列化技术在繁重的序列化工作负载下通常不灵活且效率低下，因为它们依赖于手动定义的对象模式或全功能序列化器。为了解决上述问题，我们在 Java 中揭示了一种新颖的、特定于序列化的优化机会。在此基础上，我们开发了JOSer（Just-in-time Object SERializer），一种高效的Just-in-Time（JIT）对象序列化技术。在运行时，JOSer生成一组特定于类的、JIT友好的对象序列化器（即序列化代码），然后使用Java虚拟机（JVM）的JIT编译器不断优化它们。 JOSer 还共享序列化下的对象和优化下的序列化器的元数据。我们针对六种 Java 序列化技术（包括 OpenJDK 的内置序列化技术）来评估 JOSer。总体而言，JOSer 在序列化方面将吞吐量提高了 20~83 倍，在反序列化方面提高了 43~229 倍。 JOSer 已成功部署在实际产品中，将 Flink 序列化 CPU 占用率降低 35.32~41.14%，搜索推荐延迟降低 30+ms。 JOSer 基于 Apache Fory#8482;，这是一个开源序列化框架，可在 https://fory.apache.org/ 上找到。

papers.title: LAER-MoE: Load-Adaptive Expert Re-layout for Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training.
papers.abstract:
Expert parallelism is vital for effectively training Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, enabling different devices to host distinct experts, with each device processing different input data. However, during expert parallel training, dynamic routing results in significant load imbalance among experts: a handful of overloaded experts hinder overall iteration, emerging as a training bottleneck.

In this paper, we introduce LAER-MoE, an efficient MoE training framework. The core of LAER-MoE is a novel parallel paradigm, Fully Sharded Expert Parallel (FSEP), which fully partitions each expert parameter by the number of devices and restores partial experts at expert granularity through All-to-All communication during training. This allows for flexible re-layout of expert parameters during training to enhance load balancing. In particular, we perform fine-grained scheduling of communication operations to minimize communication overhead. Additionally, we develop a load balancing planner to formulate re-layout strategies of experts and routing schemes for tokens during training. We perform experiments on an A100 cluster, and the results indicate that our system achieves up to 1.69x acceleration compared to the current state-of-the-art training systems. Source code available at https://github.com/PKU-DAIR/Hetu-Galvatron/tree/laer-moe.
papers.abstract_zh:
专家并行性对于有效训练专家混合 (MoE) 模型至关重要，使不同的设备能够托管不同的专家，每个设备处理不同的输入数据。然而，在专家并行训练过程中，动态路由会导致专家之间的负载严重不平衡：少数超载的专家阻碍了整体迭代，成为训练瓶颈。

在本文中，我们介绍了 LAER-MoE，一种高效的 MoE 训练框架。 LAER-MoE的核心是一种新颖的并行范式——全分片专家并行（FSEP），它按照设备数量完全划分每个专家参数，并在训练期间通过All-to-All通信以专家粒度恢复部分专家。这允许在训练期间灵活地重新布局专家参数，以增强负载平衡。特别是，我们对通信操作进行细粒度的调度，以最大限度地减少通信开销。此外，我们开发了一个负载平衡规划器来制定专家的重新布局策略和训练期间令牌的路由方案。我们在 A100 集群上进行了实验，结果表明，与当前最先进的训练系统相比，我们的系统实现了高达 1.69 倍的加速。源代码可在 https://github.com/PKU-DAIR/Hetu-Galvatron/tree/laer-moe 获取。

papers.title: LAIKA: Machine Learning-Assisted In-Kernel APU Acceleration.
papers.abstract:
The integration of machine learning (ML) into OS kernels is severely hampered by the high latency of offloading to discrete GPUs (dGPUs), where data transfers across the PCIe bus can consume over 93% of the total execution time. This paper argues that for many latency-sensitive kernel tasks, the solution is not a more powerful dGPU but a fundamental shift to an I/O-efficient architecture: the integrated GPU (iGPU) found in modern APUs.

We present LAIKA, a kernel-space acceleration framework that fully exploits the APU's unified memory. LAIKA combines three key mechanisms: a lightweight API proxy (AProxy) to safely bridge the kernel-user boundary, a tri-domain shared memory manager (AShm) for true zero-copy data exchange, and APU Persistent Kernel (APK) to eliminate control-path overhead. Across a suite of representative kernel workloads, our evaluation shows that by eliminating the I/O bottleneck, LAIKA slashes end-to-end inference latency by up to 9.7× and reduces total system power to as little as 28.9% of an optimized dGPU baseline. Our work identifies iGPUs as a practical and highly efficient alternative for machine learning acceleration within OS kernels.
papers.abstract_zh:
机器学习 (ML) 与操作系统内核的集成受到卸载到离散 GPU (dGPU) 的高延迟的严重阻碍，其中通过 PCIe 总线的数据传输可能会占用总执行时间的 93% 以上。本文认为，对于许多延迟敏感的内核任务，解决方案不是更强大的 dGPU，而是向 I/O 高效架构的根本转变：现代 APU 中的集成 GPU (iGPU)。

我们推出了 LAIKA，一个充分利用 APU 统一内存的内核空间加速框架。 LAIKA 结合了三种关键机制：用于安全桥接内核与用户边界的轻量级 API 代理 (AProxy)、用于真正零拷贝数据交换的三域共享内存管理器 (AShm) 以及用于消除控制路径开销的 APU 持久内核 (APK)。在一系列具有代表性的内核工作负载中，我们的评估表明，通过消除 I/O 瓶颈，LAIKA 将端到端推理延迟降低了高达 9.7 倍，并将系统总功耗降低至优化 dGPU 基准的 28.9%。我们的工作将 iGPU 确定为操作系统内核内机器学习加速的实用且高效的替代方案。

papers.title: Lifetime-Aware Design for Item-Level Intelligence at the Extreme Edge.
papers.abstract: We present FlexiFlow, a lifetime-aware design framework for item-level intelligence (ILI) where computation is integrated directly into disposable products like food packaging and medical patches. Our framework leverages natively flexible electronics which offer significantly lower costs than silicon but are limited to kHz speeds and several thousands of gates. Our insight is that unlike traditional computing with more uniform deployment patterns, ILI applications exhibit 1000× variation in operational lifetime, fundamentally changing optimal architectural design decisions when considering trillion-item deployment scales. To enable holistic design and optimization, we model the trade-offs between embodied carbon footprint and operational carbon footprint based on application-specific lifetimes. The framework includes: (1) FlexiBench, a workload suite targeting sustainability applications from spoilage detection to health monitoring; (2) FlexiBits, area-optimized RISC-V cores with 1/4/8-bit datapaths achieving 2.65× to 3.50× better energy efficiency per workload execution; and (3) a carbon-aware model that selects optimal architectures based on deployment characteristics. We show that lifetime-aware microarchitectural design can reduce carbon footprint by 1.62×, while algorithmic decisions can reduce carbon footprint by 14.5×. We validate our approach through the first tape-out using a PDK for flexible electronics with fully open-source tools, achieving 30.9\,kHz operation. FlexiFlow enables exploration of computing at the Extreme Edge where conventional design methodologies must be reevaluated to account for new constraints and considerations. FlexiFlow is available at https://github.com/harvard-edge/FlexiFlow.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们推出 FlexiFlow，这是一种用于项目级智能 (ILI) 的生命周期感知设计框架，其中计算直接集成到食品包装和医疗贴片等一次性产品中。我们的框架利用原生灵活的电子器件，其成本比硅低得多，但仅限于 kHz 速度和数千个门。我们的见解是，与具有更统一部署模式的传统计算不同，ILI 应用程序在运行生命周期中表现出 1000 倍的变化，在考虑万亿项部署规模时从根本上改变了最佳架构设计决策。为了实现整体设计和优化，我们根据特定应用的生命周期对隐含碳足迹和运营碳足迹之间的权衡进行建模。该框架包括：(1) FlexiBench，一个针对从腐败检测到健康监测等可持续应用的工作负载套件； (2) FlexiBits，面积优化的 RISC-V 内核，具有 1/4/8 位数据路径，每次工作负载执行能效提高 2.65 倍至 3.50 倍； (3) 碳感知模型，根据部署特性选择最佳架构。我们证明，生命周期感知的微架构设计可以将碳足迹减少 1.62 倍，而算法决策可以将碳足迹减少 14.5 倍。我们通过使用完全开源工具的柔性电子器件 PDK 进行首次流片来验证我们的方法，实现 30.9kHz 操作。 FlexiFlow 能够探索极端边缘的计算，必须重新评估传统的设计方法以考虑新的约束和考虑因素。 FlexiFlow 可在 https://github.com/harvard-edge/FlexiFlow 上获取。

papers.title: Linear Layouts: Robust Code Generation of Efficient Tensor Computation Using F_2.
papers.abstract: Efficient tensor computation is a cornerstone of modern deep learning (DL) workloads, yet existing approaches struggle to achieve flexible and performant design and implementation of tensor layouts -- mappings between logical tensors and hardware resources. The increasing complexity of DL algorithms and hardware demands a generic and systematic approach to handling tensor layouts. In this work, we introduce Linear Layouts, a novel approach that models tensor layouts using linear algebra over F 2 . By representing tensor layouts as binary matrices acting on the bits of the hardware representation, our approach enables a generic layout definition—as opposed to the classical case-by-case approach -- and allows for generic layout-to-layout conversions, eliminating the quadratic explosion that plagues existing solutions. We integrate linear layouts with Triton and demonstrate their effectiveness in optimizing individual Triton operators as well as kernels written in Triton. We also show that linear layouts reduce engineering effort in the compiler backend while fixing several bugs in Triton's legacy layout system.
papers.abstract_zh: 高效的张量计算是现代深度学习 (DL) 工作负载的基石，但现有方法难以实现张量布局（逻辑张量与硬件资源之间的映射）的灵活且高性能的设计和实现。深度学习算法和硬件日益复杂，需要一种通用且系统的方法来处理张量布局。在这项工作中，我们介绍了线性布局，这是一种使用 F 2 上的线性代数对张量布局进行建模的新颖方法。通过将张量布局表示为作用于硬件表示位的二进制矩阵，我们的方法可以实现通用布局定义（与经典的逐例方法相反），并允许通用布局到布局的转换，消除困扰现有解决方案的二次爆炸。我们将线性布局与 Triton 集成，并展示了它们在优化各个 Triton 运算符以及用 Triton 编写的内核方面的有效性。我们还表明，线性布局减少了编译器后端的工程工作量，同时修复了 Triton 遗留布局系统中的几个错误。

papers.title: Lobster: A GPU-Accelerated Framework for Neurosymbolic Programming.
papers.abstract:
Neurosymbolic programs combine deep learning with symbolic reasoning to achieve better data efficiency, interpretability, and generalizability compared to standalone deep learning approaches. However, existing neurosymbolic learning frameworks implement an uneasy marriage between a highly scalable, GPU-accelerated neural component and a slower symbolic component that runs on CPUs.

We propose Lobster, a unified framework for harnessing GPUs in an end-to-end manner for neurosymbolic learning. Lobster maps a general neurosymbolic language based on Datalog to the GPU programming paradigm. This mapping is implemented via compilation to a new intermediate language called APM. The extra abstraction provided by APM allows Lobster to be both flexible, supporting discrete, probabilistic, and differentiable modes of reasoning on GPU hardware with a library of provenance semirings, and performant, implementing new optimization passes.

We demonstrate that Lobster programs can solve interesting problems spanning the domains of natural language processing, image processing, program reasoning, bioinformatics, and planning. On a suite of 9 applications, Lobster achieves an average speedup of 3.9x over Scallop, a state-of-the-art neurosymbolic framework, and enables scaling of neurosymbolic solutions to previously infeasible tasks.
papers.abstract_zh:
与独立的深度学习方法相比，神经符号程序将深度学习与符号推理相结合，以实现更好的数据效率、可解释性和泛化性。然而，现有的神经符号学习框架在高度可扩展、GPU 加速的神经组件和在 CPU 上运行的较慢的符号组件之间实现了不稳定的结合。

我们提出了 Lobster，这是一个以端到端方式利用 GPU 进行神经符号学习的统一框架。 Lobster 将基于 Datalog 的通用神经符号语言映射到 GPU 编程范例。这种映射是通过编译为一种称为 APM 的新中间语言来实现的。 APM 提供的额外抽象使 Lobster 既灵活，通过来源半环库支持 GPU 硬件上的离散、概率和可微的推理模式，又具有高性能，实现新的优化过程。

我们证明 Lobster 程序可以解决自然语言处理、图像处理、程序推理、生物信息学和规划领域的有趣问题。在 9 个应用程序套件中，Lobster 比最先进的神经符号框架 Scallop 平均加速了 3.9 倍，并且能够将神经符号解决方案扩展到以前不可行的任务。

papers.title: LOOPRAG: Enhancing Loop Transformation Optimization with Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models.
papers.abstract:
Loop transformations are semantics-preserving optimization techniques applied at the source level, widely used in compilers to maximize objectives such as vectorization and parallelism. Despite decades of research, applying the optimal composition of loop transformations remains challenging due to inherent complexities, including dependency analysis and cost modeling for optimization objectives.

Recent studies have explored the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) for code optimization. However, our key observation is that LLMs often struggle with effective loop transformation optimization, frequently leading to errors or suboptimal optimization, thereby missing significant opportunities for performance improvements.

To bridge this gap, we propose LOOPRAG, a novel retrieval-augmented generation framework designed to guide LLMs in performing effective loop optimization on Static Control Part (SCoP). We introduce a parameter-driven method to harness loop properties, which trigger various loop transformations, and generate diverse yet legal example codes serving as a demonstration source. To effectively obtain the most informative demonstrations, we propose a loop-aware algorithm based on loop features, which balances similarity and diversity for code retrieval. To enhance correct and efficient code generation, we introduce a feedback-based iterative mechanism that incorporates compilation, testing and performance results as feedback to guide LLMs. Each optimized code generated by LOOPRAG undergoes mutation, coverage and differential testing to perform equivalence checking.

We evaluate LOOPRAG on PolyBench, TSVC and LORE benchmark suites, and compare it against compilers (GCC-Graphite, Clang-Polly, Perspective and ICX) and representative LLMs (DeepSeek and GPT-4). The results demonstrate average speedups over compilers of up to 11.20×, 14.34×, and 9.29× for PolyBench, TSVC, and LORE, respectively, and speedups over base LLMs of up to 11.97×, 5.61×, and 11.59×.
papers.abstract_zh:
循环转换是在源代码级别应用的语义保留优化技术，广泛用于编译器中以最大化矢量化和并行性等目标。尽管经过数十年的研究，由于固有的复杂性，应用循环变换的最佳组合仍然具有挑战性，包括优化目标的依赖性分析和成本建模。

最近的研究探索了大型语言模型 (LLM) 在代码优化方面的潜力。然而，我们的主要观察结果是，法学硕士经常在有效的循环转换优化方面遇到困难，经常导致错误或次优优化，从而错过了提高性能的重要机会。

为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了 LOOPRAG，这是一种新颖的检索增强生成框架，旨在指导法学硕士在静态控制部分（SCoP）上执行有效的循环优化。我们引入了一种参数驱动的方法来利用循环属性，该方法触发各种循环转换，并生成各种但合法的示例代码作为演示源。为了有效地获得信息最丰富的演示，我们提出了一种基于循环特征的循环感知算法，该算法平衡了代码检索的相似性和多样性。为了增强正确和高效的代码生成，我们引入了一种基于反馈的迭代机制，将编译、测试和性能结果作为反馈来指导法学硕士。 LOOPRAG 生成的每个优化代码都会经过变异、覆盖和差异测试以执行等效性检查。

我们在 PolyBench、TSVC 和 LORE 基准测试套件上评估 LOOPRAG，并将其与编译器（GCC-Graphite、Clang-Polly、Perspective 和 ICX）和代表性 LLM（DeepSeek 和 GPT-4）进行比较。结果表明，PolyBench、TSVC 和 LORE 的编译器平均加速分别高达 11.20×、14.34× 和 9.29×，而基础 LLM 的平均加速高达 11.97×、5.61× 和 11.59×。

papers.title: LPO: Discovering Missed Peephole Optimizations with Large Language Models.
papers.abstract:
Peephole optimization is an essential class of compiler optimizations that targets small, inefficient instruction sequences within programs. By replacing such suboptimal instructions with refined and more optimal sequences, these optimizations not only directly optimize code size and performance, but also enable more transformations in the subsequent optimization pipeline. Despite their importance, discovering new and effective peephole optimizations remains challenging due to the complexity and breadth of instruction sets. Prior approaches either lack scalability or have significant restrictions on the peephole optimizations that they can find.

This paper introduces LPO, a novel automated framework to discover missed peephole optimizations. Our key insight is that, Large Language Models (LLMs) are effective at creative exploration but susceptible to hallucinations; conversely, formal verification techniques provide rigorous guarantees but struggle with creative discovery. By synergistically combining the strengths of LLMs and formal verifiers in a closed-loop feedback mechanism, LPO can effectively discover verified peephole optimizations that were previously missed.

We comprehensively evaluated LPO within LLVM ecosystems. Our evaluation shows that LPO can successfully identify up to 22 out of 25 previously reported missed optimizations in LLVM. In contrast, the recently proposed superoptimizers for LLVM, Souper and Minotaur detected 15 and 3 of them, respectively. More importantly, within eleven months of development and intermittent testing, LPO found 62 missed peephole optimizations, of which 28 were confirmed and an additional 13 had already been fixed in LLVM. These results demonstrate LPO's strong potential to continuously uncover new optimizations as LLMs' reasoning improves.
papers.abstract_zh:
窥孔优化是一类重要的编译器优化，针对程序中小型、低效的指令序列。通过用精炼和更优化的序列替换此类次优指令，这些优化不仅可以直接优化代码大小和性能，而且可以在后续优化管道中实现更多转换。尽管它们很重要，但由于指令集的复杂性和广度，发现新的有效的窥孔优化仍然具有挑战性。先前的方法要么缺乏可扩展性，要么对它们可以找到的窥孔优化有很大的限制。

本文介绍了 LPO，一种新颖的自动化框架，用于发现错过的窥孔优化。我们的主要见解是，大型语言模型（LLM）在创造性探索方面很有效，但容易产生幻觉；相反，形式验证技术提供严格的保证，但难以创造性发现。通过在闭环反馈机制中协同结合 LLM 和形式验证者的优势，LPO 可以有效地发现之前错过的经过验证的窥孔优化。

我们全面评估了 LLVM 生态系统中的 LPO。我们的评估表明，LPO 可以成功识别 LLVM 中先前报告的 25 个错过的优化中的多达 22 个。相比之下，最近提出的 LLVM、Souper 和 Minotaur 超级优化器分别检测到了 15 个和 3 个。更重要的是，在开发和间歇测试的 11 个月内，LPO 发现了 62 个错过的窥孔优化，其中 28 个已得到确认，另外 13 个已在 LLVM 中修复。这些结果表明，随着法学硕士推理能力的提高，LPO 具有不断发现新优化的强大潜力。

papers.title: M2XFP: A Metadata-Augmented Microscaling Data Format for Efficient Low-bit Quantization.
papers.abstract: Existing low-bit Microscaling (MX) formats, such as MXFP4, often suffer from substantial accuracy degradation due to the use of a shared scaling factor with the Power-of-Two format. In this work, we explore strategies that introduce minimal metadata to recover accuracy lost during quantization while maintaining high bit efficiency across a wide range of large language models. We propose a complete algorithm-hardware co-design based on flexible metadata, featuring an online quantization with simple encoding. To support the proposed method efficiently, we implement a lightweight hardware unit and integrate it into the accelerator. Evaluation results demonstrate that our method substantially narrows the accuracy gap, achieving on average a 70.63% reduction in accuracy loss compared to MXFP4 and a 37.30% reduction relative to the latest NVFP4 on LLM benchmarks. Furthermore, our design delivers up to 1.91× speedup and 1.75× energy savings over state-of-the-art accelerators.
papers.abstract_zh: 现有的低位微缩放 (MX) 格式（例如 MXFP4）通常会因使用与二次方格式共享的缩放因子而导致精度大幅下降。在这项工作中，我们探索了引入最少元数据的策略，以恢复量化过程中丢失的准确性，同时在各种大型语言模型中保持高位效率。我们提出了一种基于灵活元数据的完整算法-硬件协同设计，具有简单编码的在线量化功能。为了有效地支持所提出的方法，我们实现了一个轻量级硬件单元并将其集成到加速器中。评估结果表明，我们的方法大大缩小了精度差距，与 MXFP4 相比，精度损失平均降低了 70.63%，与 LLM 基准上最新的 NVFP4 相比，精度损失平均降低了 37.30%。此外，与最先进的加速器相比，我们的设计可实现高达 1.91 倍的加速和 1.75 倍的节能。

papers.title: Maverick: Rethinking TFHE Bootstrapping on GPUs via Algorithm-Hardware Co-Design.
papers.abstract:
Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) enables arbitrary computation over encrypted data (ciphertext) without compromising confidentiality. Within this family, TFHE features versatile bootstrapping mechanisms that is attractive for security-critical applications. However, its prohibitive computational cost severely limits practical deployment. While hardware acceleration is promising, mere compute scaling fails to overcome the inherent barriers. In particular, the combination of limited algorithmic parallelism and inadequate understanding of hardware behaviors prevents full exploitation of the available performance headroom.

In this paper, we present Maverick, a GPU-based solution that rethinks TFHE bootstrapping acceleration through joint algorithm–hardware co-design. At the algorithmic level, we reconstruct blind rotation by shifting test vectors from pre-loading to post-injection. This reformulation turns the original length- n single chain into a multi-chain structure with √ n sub-chains of depth √ n , raising external-product (EP) parallelism from 1 to the theoretical bound of √ n . At the hardware level, we introduce a partial-domain transformation strategy that terminates the NTT early in a stage-aware manner and selectively shifts computation to downstream operators, redefining operator boundaries to balance the workload across EP operators. Experimental results show that Maverick delivers state-of-the-art GPU acceleration. It outperforms the best existing CPU and GPU baselines by 331.2× and 3.4×, respectively, on programmable bootstrapping, and achieves up to 108.5× speedup over CPU-based circuit bootstrapping. Together, these results highlight the substantial potential of algorithm–hardware co-design for advancing FHE performance on general-purpose platforms.
papers.abstract_zh:
完全同态加密 (FHE) 可以在不影响机密性的情况下对加密数据（密文）进行任意计算。在该系列中，TFHE 具有多功能引导机制，对于安全关键应用程序很有吸引力。然而，其高昂的计算成本严重限制了实际部署。虽然硬件加速前景广阔，但单纯的计算扩展无法克服固有的障碍。特别是，有限的算法并行性和对硬件行为的不充分理解相结合，阻碍了可用性能空间的充分利用。

在本文中，我们提出了 Maverick，一种基于 GPU 的解决方案，通过联合算法-硬件协同设计重新思考 TFHE 自举加速。在算法层面，我们通过将测试向量从预加载转移到注入后来重建盲旋转。这种重新表述将原始长度为 n 的单链变成了具有 √ n 个深度为 √ n 的子链的多链结构，将外积（EP）并行度从 1 提高到了 √ n 的理论界限。在硬件层面，我们引入了部分域转换策略，该策略以阶段感知的方式提前终止 NTT，并有选择地将计算转移到下游算子，重新定义算子边界以平衡 EP 算子之间的工作负载。实验结果表明 Maverick 提供最先进的 GPU 加速。在可编程引导方面，它的性能分别比现有最佳 CPU 和 GPU 基线高出 331.2 倍和 3.4 倍，并且比基于 CPU 的电路引导实现高达 108.5 倍的加速。总之，这些结果凸显了算法-硬件协同设计在提高通用平台上的 FHE 性能方面的巨大潜力。

papers.title: Mission-Critical Enterprise Systems... What's a Mission? And What's Critical?: Processor and technology requirements for enterprise computing.
papers.abstract:
Behind the scenes, mainframes (IBM Z) and IBM Power Systems fuel banking, manufacturing, healthcare, insurance, transportation, and many other critical industries. While we each touch multiple silicon-based systems daily, enterprise systems are the ones you never see, but always rely on. In this talk, we'll look inside these systems, discuss the silicon technology, microarchitecture, and system elements which come together to deliver eight nines of system-level resiliency (99.999999% uptime). We'll explore the changes in workloads on these systems, and discuss how new drivers like AI for fraud detection, AI for insurance claims processing, and AI-driven system operations are changing enterprise requirements.

Bio: Hillery is the General Manager for IBM Power and serves as IBM Infrastructure CTO. In this role, she is focused on setting the global strategy for both Power and Infrastructure Platform in addition to driving cross-functional innovation and growth across IBM's Infrastructure portfolio which includes IBM zSystems, Power, Storage, TLS and IBM Cloud. In her previous roles as the GM, Industry Clouds and CTO of IBM Cloud, she led the business and technical strategy for IBM's industry-contextualized cloud offerings and was responsible for technical strategy for IBM's public cloud.
