To take advantage of the increased concurrent rays, we propose a dynamic treelet queue architecture that dynamically switches between traversal modes to increase efficiency. We also find that performing warp repacking boosts SIMT efficiency of warps in the RT unit which is crucial to achieving good traversal performance with treelet queues. Our simulations show virtualized treelet queues achieve on average 95% speedup compared to a baseline GPU with ray tracing acceleration across all scenes in LumiBench rendered with path tracing at one sample per pixel with three max bounces per ray.
papers.abstract_zh: 尽管硬件加速取得了进步，但光线追踪在实时渲染中的使用仍然有限，并且通常会降低帧速率，导致视频游戏玩家等用户完全禁用该功能。先前的工作表明，将 BVH 树划分为较小的子树（小树）并在切换小树之前遍历访问小树的所有光线可以显着减少专用加速器上的内存流量，但将小树应用于 GPU 存在许多挑战。我们发现简单的小树实现是无效的，并提出了优化来提高性能。虚拟化 Treelet 队列由两个主要组件组成。光线虚拟化通过终止已发出跟踪光线指令的 raygen 着色器、回收 CUDA 核心并允许执行更多 raygen 着色器，增加了运行中的并发光线数量，从而创建更多缓存重用机会。为了利用增加的并发光线，我们提出了一种动态小树队列架构，可以在遍历模式之间动态切换以提高效率。我们还发现，执行扭曲重新打包可以提高 RT 单元中扭曲的 SIMT 效率，这对于通过小树队列实现良好的遍历性能至关重要。我们的模拟显示，与基准 GPU 相比，虚拟化小树队列平均实现 95% 的加速，并且在 LumiBench 中的所有场景中提供光线追踪加速，并通过每像素一个样本的路径追踪以及每条光线最多 3 次反弹进行渲染。

papers.title: UniZK: Accelerating Zero-Knowledge Proof with Unified Hardware and Flexible Kernel Mapping.
papers.abstract: Zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) is an important cryptographic tool that sees wide applications in real-world scenarios where privacy must be protected, including privacy-preserving blockchains and zero-knowledge machine learning. Existing ZKP acceleration approaches using GPUs, FPGAs, and ASICs focus only on classic protocols that rely on expensive elliptic curve arithmetics. Emerging ZKP protocols based on hash functions can greatly reduce the algorithmic complexity, but they also introduce much more diverse computation kernels that cannot be efficiently handled by a single accelerator chip if dedicated units for each kernel are used. Our approach is to leverage a unified hardware architecture that is able to efficiently support the common primitives in ZKP, and then use smart mapping strategies to flexibly map various kernels to such hardware while ensuring high resource utilization. We design UniZK as such a ZKP accelerator, with a systolic-array-based hardware architecture enhanced with extra local links and a new vector processing mode. We propose novel mapping strategies to support diverse kernels including number theoretic transforms, hash functions, and general polynomial computations. UniZK provides 97x and 46x speedups on average compared to the CPU and GPU implementations of the same protocols, and is also 840x faster than previous ZKP accelerators using different protocols.
papers.abstract_zh: 零知识证明（ZKP）是一种重要的密码学工具，在必须保护隐私的现实场景中有着广泛的应用，包括隐私保护区块链和零知识机器学习。现有的使用 GPU、FPGA 和 ASIC 的 ZKP 加速方法仅关注依赖昂贵的椭圆曲线算法的经典协议。新兴的基于哈希函数的 ZKP 协议可以大大降低算法复杂性，但它们也引入了更加多样化的计算内核，如果每个内核使用专用单元，则单个加速器芯片无法有效处理这些内核。我们的方法是利用统一的硬件架构，能够有效地支持ZKP中的通用原语，然后使用智能映射策略将各种内核灵活地映射到此类硬件，同时确保高资源利用率。我们将 UniZK 设计为这样的 ZKP 加速器，具有基于脉动阵列的硬件架构，并通过额外的本地链接和新的矢量处理模式进行了增强。我们提出了新颖的映射策略来支持不同的内核，包括数论变换、哈希函数和一般多项式计算。与相同协议的 CPU 和 GPU 实现相比，UniZK 平均提供 97 倍和 46 倍的加速，并且比以前使用不同协议的 ZKP 加速器快 840 倍。

papers.title: Using Analytical Performance/Power Model and Fine-Grained DVFS to Enhance AI Accelerator Energy Efficiency.
papers.abstract:
Recent advancements in deep learning have significantly increased AI processors' energy consumption, which is becoming a critical factor limiting AI development. Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS) stands as a key method in power optimization. However, due to the latency of DVFS control in AI processors, previous works typically apply DVFS control at the granularity of a program's entire duration or sub-phases, rather than at the level of AI operators.

The advent of millisecond-level DVFS capabilities on the latest Ascend NPU platforms enables us to set frequency individually for single or multiple operators, opening up the opportunity for further enhancing energy efficiency through fine-grained DVFS control. To ensure performance is unaffected in DVFS, our work builds performance and power models for each operator. Through in-depth timeline analysis, we demonstrate that the cycle count of an operator can be modeled as a convex piecewise linear function of frequency, resulting in a performance model with an average error of 1.96%. Moreover, we build power models that incorporate temperature-dependent terms, which enhances the model's precision and results in an average error of 4.62%.

Based on our performance and power models as well as the fine-grained DVFS functionality of Ascend NPU, we propose a DVFS strategy that integrates operator classification, preprocessing, and a genetic algorithm-based search. Experiments on applications including GPT-3 training achieve a reduction in AICore (the computing component within the Ascend NPU) power by 13.44% and NPU chip power by 4.95%, while limiting performance degradation to 1.76%.
papers.abstract_zh:
深度学习的最新进展显着增加了人工智能处理器的能耗，这正成为限制人工智能发展的关键因素。动态电压和频率调节（DVFS）是功率优化的关键方法。然而，由于人工智能处理器中DVFS控制的延迟，以前的工作通常在程序的整个持续时间或子阶段的粒度上应用DVFS控制，而不是在AI算子的层面上。

最新升腾NPU平台上毫秒级DVFS功能的出现使我们能够为单个或多个操作员单独设置频率，从而为通过细粒度的DVFS控制进一步提高能源效率提供了机会。为了确保 DVFS 中的性能不受影响，我们的工作为每个操作员构建了性能和功耗模型。通过深入的时间线分析，我们证明操作员的周期计数可以建模为频率的凸分段线性函数，从而得到平均误差为 1.96% 的性能模型。此外，我们构建了包含温度相关项的功率模型，这提高了模型的精度，平均误差为 4.62%。

基于我们的性能和功耗模型以及Ascend NPU的细粒度DVFS功能，我们提出了一种集成算子分类、预处理和基于遗传算法的搜索的DVFS策略。在包括 GPT-3 训练在内的应用实验中，AICore（Ascend NPU 内的计算组件）功耗降低了 13.44%，NPU 芯片功耗降低了 4.95%，同时将性能下降限制在 1.76%。

papers.title: vAttention: Dynamic Memory Management for Serving LLMs without PagedAttention.
papers.abstract:
PagedAttention is a popular approach for dynamic memory allocation in LLM serving systems. It enables on-demand allocation of GPU memory to mitigate KV cache fragmentation - a phenomenon that crippled the batch size (and consequently throughput) in prior systems. However, in trying to allocate physical memory at runtime, PagedAttention ends up changing the virtual memory layout of the KV cache from contiguous to non-contiguous. Such a design leads to non-trivial programming and performance overheads.

We present vAttention - an approach that mitigates fragmentation in physical memory while retaining the virtual memory contiguity of the KV cache. We achieve this by decoupling the allocation of virtual and physical memory using CUDA virtual memory management APIs. We also introduce various LLM-specific optimizations to address the limitations of CUDA virtual memory support. Overall, vAttention is a simpler, portable, and performant alternative to PagedAttention: it supports various attention kernels out-of-the-box and improves LLM serving throughput by up to 1.23× compared to the use of PagedAttention-based kernels of FlashAttention-2 and FlashInfer.
papers.abstract_zh:
PagedAttention 是 LLM 服务系统中动态内存分配的一种流行方法。它支持按需分配 GPU 内存，以减轻 KV 缓存碎片——这种现象会削弱先前系统中的批量大小（进而削弱吞吐量）。然而，在运行时尝试分配物理内存时，PagedAttention 最终会将 KV 缓存的虚拟内存布局从连续更改为非连续。这样的设计会导致不平凡的编程和性能开销。

我们提出了 vAttention - 一种减少物理内存碎片的方法，同时保留 KV 缓存的虚拟内存连续性。我们通过使用 CUDA 虚拟内存管理 API 解耦虚拟内存和物理内存的分配来实现这一目标。我们还引入了各种 LLM 特定的优化来解决 CUDA 虚拟内存支持的限制。总体而言，vAttention 是 PagedAttention 的更简单、可移植且高性能的替代方案：与使用基于 PagedAttention 的 FlashAttention-2 和 FlashInfer 内核相比，它支持开箱即用的各种注意力内核，并将 LLM 服务吞吐量提高了 1.23 倍。

papers.title: Vela: A Virtualized LLM Training System with GPU Direct RoCE.
papers.abstract: Vela is a cloud-native system designed for LLM training workloads built using off-the-shelf hardware, Linux KVM-based virtualization, and a virtualized RDMA over Converged Ethernet (RoCE) network. Vela virtual machines (VMs) support peer-to-peer DMA between the GPUs and SRIOV-based network interface. In this paper, we share Vela's key architectural aspects with details from an NVIDIA A100 GPU-based deployment in one of the IBM Cloud data centers. Throughout the paper, we share insights and experiences from designing, building, and operating the system over a ~2.5 year timeframe to highlight the capabilities of readily available software and hardware technologies and the improvement opportunities for future AI systems, thereby making AI infrastructure more accessible to a broader community. As we evaluated the system for performance at ~1500 GPU scale, we achieved ~80% of the ideal throughput while training a 50 billion parameter decoder model using model parallelism, and ~70% per GPU FLOPS compared to a single VM with the High-Performance Linpack benchmark.
papers.abstract_zh: Vela 是一款专为 LLM 培训工作负载而设计的云原生系统，使用现成的硬件、基于 Linux KVM 的虚拟化和基于融合以太网 (RoCE) 网络的虚拟化 RDMA 构建。 Vela 虚拟机 (VM) 支持 GPU 和基于 SRIOV 的网络接口之间的点对点 DMA。在本文中，我们将分享 Vela 的关键架构方面以及 IBM Cloud 数据中心之一中基于 NVIDIA A100 GPU 的部署的详细信息。在整篇论文中，我们分享了在大约 2.5 年的时间范围内设计、构建和运营该系统的见解和经验，以突出现成的软件和硬件技术的功能以及未来人工智能系统的改进机会，从而使人工智能基础设施更容易为更广泛的社区所使用。当我们评估系统在约 1500 个 GPU 规模的性能时，我们在使用模型并行性训练 500 亿个参数解码器模型时实现了约 80% 的理想吞吐量，与使用高性能 Linpack 基准测试的单个虚拟机相比，每 GPU FLOPS 达到了约 70%。

papers.title: Velosiraptor: Code Synthesis for Memory Translation.
papers.abstract:
Security is among the top concerns of operating system (OS) developers. A secure runtime environment relies on the OS to correctly configure the memory hardware on which it runs. This is mission-critical as it provides essential security-relevant features and abstractions that ensure the integrity and isolation of untrusted applications running alongside each other. Configuring a platform's memory hardware is not a one-off effort as designers constantly develop new mechanisms for translation and protection with different features and means of configuration. Adapting the OS code to the new hardware is not only a manual, repetitive and time-consuming task, it may also introduce subtle, but security-critical bugs that break security and isolation guarantees.

We present Velosiraptor , a system that automatically generates correct, low-level OS code that programs the memory hardware of a machine. Velosiraptor leverages software synthesis techniques and exploits the domain specificity of the problem to make the synthesis process efficient. With Velosiraptor , developers write only a high-level description of the memory hardware's mapping behavior and OS environment.

The Velosiraptor toolchain transforms this specification into a verified implementation that can be linked directly with the rest of the operating system. Incorporating the OS environment into this process allows porting an OS to new hardware platforms without worrying about writing code to configure the memory hardware. We can also use the same specification to generate hardware components. This enables research in new translation mechanisms, freeing up OS developers from manually writing OS code.
papers.abstract_zh:
安全性是操作系统 (OS) 开发人员最关心的问题之一。安全的运行时环境依赖于操作系统正确配置其运行的内存硬件。这是至关重要的，因为它提供了基本的安全相关功能和抽象，可确保彼此运行的不受信任应用程序的完整性和隔离性。配置平台的内存硬件并不是一次性的工作，因为设计人员不断开发具有不同功能和配置方式的新转换和保护机制。使操作系统代码适应新硬件不仅是一项手动、重复且耗时的任务，还可能引入微妙但对安全至关重要的错误，从而破坏安全性和隔离保证。

我们推出了 Velosiraptor，这是一个可以自动生成正确的低级操作系统代码的系统，该代码可以对机器的内存硬件进行编程。 Velosiraptor 利用软件综合技术并利用问题的领域特异性来提高综合过程的效率。使用 Velosiraptor，开发人员只需编写内存硬件映射行为和操作系统环境的高级描述。

Velosiraptor 工具链将此规范转换为经过验证的实现，可以直接与操作系统的其余部分链接。将操作系统环境合并到此过程中可以将操作系统移植到新的硬件平台，而无需担心编写代码来配置内存硬件。我们还可以使用相同的规格来生成硬件组件。这使得能够研究新的翻译机制，使操作系统开发人员从手动编写操作系统代码中解放出来。

papers.title: Virgo: Cluster-level Matrix Unit Integration in GPUs for Scalability and Energy Efficiency.
papers.abstract:
Modern GPUs incorporate specialized matrix units such as Tensor Cores to accelerate GEMM operations, which are central to deep learning workloads. However, existing matrix unit designs are tightly coupled to the SIMT core, restricting operation size due to register file capacity and bandwidth constraints. Such a limitation in scalability makes it difficult to simultaneously improve compute throughput and energy efficiency in GPUs.

To address this challenge, we propose Virgo, a GPU microarchitecture that integrates dedicated matrix units at the SIMT core cluster level. By decoupling the matrix unit from the SIMT core, Virgo eliminates scalability constraints imposed by the core microarchitecture. Consequently, Virgo increases operation granularity at the hardware level, reducing energy overhead from core instruction processing. Physical disaggregation also enables a unified matrix unit design and offloading both operand and accumulator accesses from the register file, improving data reuse and energy efficiency. Furthermore, this disaggregation supports efficient concurrent execution of the SIMT core and matrix unit, optimizing mapping for fused DNN workloads. Our evaluations using synthesizable RTL demonstrate that Virgo achieves 67.3% and 24.2% reduction in on-chip active power consumption, compared to the baseline Ampere-style and Hopper-style core-coupled designs.
papers.abstract_zh:
现代 GPU 结合了 Tensor Core 等专用矩阵单元来加速 GEMM 运算，这对于深度学习工作负载至关重要。然而，现有的矩阵单元设计与 SIMT 核心紧密耦合，由于寄存器文件容量和带宽限制而限制了操作大小。这种可扩展性的限制使得同时提高 GPU 的计算吞吐量和能源效率变得困难。

为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了 Virgo，一种在 SIMT 核心集群级别集成专用矩阵单元的 GPU 微架构。通过将矩阵单元与 SIMT 核心解耦，Virgo 消除了核心微架构带来的可扩展性限制。因此，Virgo 增加了硬件级别的操作粒度，减少了核心指令处理的能量开销。物理分解还支持统一的矩阵单元设计，并从寄存器文件中卸载操作数和累加器访问，从而提高数据重用和能源效率。此外，这种分解支持 SIMT 核心和矩阵单元的高效并发执行，优化融合 DNN 工作负载的映射。我们使用可综合 RTL 进行的评估表明，与基准 Ampere 型和 Hopper 型核心耦合设计相比，Virgo 的片上有功功耗分别降低了 67.3% 和 24.2%。

papers.title: Virtuoso: Enabling Fast and Accurate Virtual Memory Research via an Imitation-based Operating System Simulation Methodology.
papers.abstract:
The unprecedented growth in data demand from emerging applications has turned virtual memory (VM) into a major performance bottleneck. VM's overheads are expected to persist as memory requirements continue to increase. Researchers explore new hardware/OS co-designs to optimize VM across diverse applications and systems. To evaluate such designs, researchers rely on various simulation methodologies to model VM components. Unfortunately, current simulation tools (i) either lack the desired accuracy in modeling VM's software components or (ii) are too slow and complex to prototype and evaluate schemes that span across the hardware/software boundary.

We introduce Virtuoso, a new simulation framework that enables quick and accurate prototyping and evaluation of the software and hardware components of the VM subsystem. The key idea of Virtuoso is to employ a lightweight userspace OS kernel , called MimicOS, that (i) accelerates simulation time by imitating only the desired kernel functionalities, (ii) facilitates the development of new OS routines that imitate real ones, using an accessible high-level programming interface, (iii) enables accurate and flexible evaluation of the application- and system-level implications of VM after integrating Virtuoso to a desired architectural simulator.

In this work, we integrate Virtuoso into five diverse architectural simulators, each specializing in different aspects of system design, and heavily enrich it with multiple state-of-the-art VM schemes. This way, we establish a common ground for researchers to evaluate current VM designs and to develop and test new ones. We demonstrate Virtuoso's flexibility and versatility by evaluating five diverse use cases, yielding new insights into state-of-the-art VM techniques. Our validation shows that Virtuoso ported on top of Sniper, a state-of-the-art microarchitectural simulator, models (i) the memory management unit of a real high-end server-grade CPU with 82% accuracy, and (ii) the page fault latency of a real Linux kernel with up to 79% accuracy. Consequently, Virtuoso models the IPC performance of a real high-end server-grade CPU with 21% higher accuracy than the baseline version of Sniper. Virtuoso's accuracy benefits incur an average simulation time overhead of only 20%, on top of four baseline architectural simulators. The source code of Virtuoso is freely available at https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/Virtuoso.
papers.abstract_zh:
新兴应用程序的数据需求空前增长，使虚拟内存 (VM) 成为主要的性能瓶颈。随着内存需求的不断增加，虚拟机的开销预计将持续存在。研究人员探索新的硬件/操作系统协同设计，以跨不同应用程序和系统优化虚拟机。为了评估此类设计，研究人员依靠各种仿真方法对 VM 组件进行建模。不幸的是，当前的仿真工具（i）要么在虚拟机软件组件建模方面缺乏所需的准确性，要么（ii）速度太慢且太复杂，无法对跨越硬件/软件边界的方案进行原型设计和评估。

我们推出了 Virtuoso，这是一种新的仿真框架，可以快速准确地对 VM 子系统的软件和硬件组件进行原型设计和评估。 Virtuoso 的关键思想是采用名为 MimicOS 的轻量级用户空间操作系统内核，它 (i) 通过仅模仿所需的内核功能来加速仿真时间，(ii) 使用可访问的高级编程接口，促进模仿真实操作系统例程的新操作系统例程的开发，(iii) 在将 Virtuoso 集成到所需的架构模拟器后，能够准确、灵活地评估 VM 的应用程序级和系统级影响。

在这项工作中，我们将 Virtuoso 集成到五个不同的架构模拟器中，每个模拟器专门从事系统设计的不同方面，并通过多种最先进的 VM 方案大大丰富了它。通过这种方式，我们为研究人员评估当前的虚拟机设计以及开发和测试新的虚拟机设计建立了一个共同点。我们通过评估五个不同的用例来展示 Virtuoso 的灵活性和多功能性，从而对最先进的 VM 技术产生新的见解。我们的验证表明，Virtuoso 移植到最先进的微架构模拟器 Sniper 之上，可以对 (i) 真实高端服务器级 CPU 的内存管理单元进行建模，准确度为 82%；(ii) 真实 Linux 内核的页面错误延迟，准确度高达 79%。因此，Virtuoso 对真正的高端服务器级 CPU 的 IPC 性能进行了建模，其准确度比 Sniper 的基准版本高 21%。与四个基准架构模拟器相比，Virtuoso 的准确性优势带来的平均模拟时间开销仅为 20%。 Virtuoso 的源代码可在 https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/Virtuoso 上免费获取。

papers.title: Voyager: Input-Adaptive Algebraic Transformations for High-Performance Graph Neural Networks.
papers.abstract:
Graph neural networks (GNNs) are gaining popularity in diverse application domains and growing in complexity. As a result, it is crucial to achieve high-performance GNN execution. Among various techniques, algebraic transformations, including operator reordering and operator fusion, have been successfully applied to improve the computation and memory access efficiencies of DNN models. However, directly applying these methods to GNNs results in suboptimal performance. This is primarily due to the diversity of input graph structures, making it challenging to predict performance benefits. Additionally, the complex dependencies introduced by these input graph structures limit the effectiveness of existing optimization techniques.

To address these challenges, we propose Voyager , an input-adaptive compiler designed specifically for GNNs. Unlike existing methods that rely on static optimization rules, Voyager dynamically generates and evaluates a wide range of algebraic transformation candidates, including novel reordering and fusion strategies tailored to graph characteristics. By leveraging symbolic graph attributes, Voyager efficiently estimates tensor shapes and prunes low-performance candidates. A runtime selection module further enhances performance by choosing the optimal transformation based on real-time input features. Our evaluation shows that Voyager achieves an average 3.5 × speedup on GPUs over the state-of-the-art works on representative GNNs and datasets.
papers.abstract_zh:
图神经网络（GNN）在不同的应用领域中越来越受欢迎，并且变得越来越复杂。因此，实现高性能 GNN 执行至关重要。在各种技术中，包括算子重排序和算子融合在内的代数变换已成功应用于提高 DNN 模型的计算和内存访问效率。然而，直接将这些方法应用于 GNN 会导致性能不佳。这主要是由于输入图结构的多样性，使得预测性能优势变得具有挑战性。此外，这些输入图结构引入的复杂依赖关系限制了现有优化技术的有效性。

为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 Voyager，这是一种专为 GNN 设计的输入自适应编译器。与依赖静态优化规则的现有方法不同，Voyager 动态生成并评估各种代数变换候选对象，包括针对图特征定制的新颖的重新排序和融合策略。通过利用符号图属性，Voyager 可以有效地估计张量形状并修剪低性能候选者。运行时选择模块通过根据实时输入特征选择最佳转换来进一步增强性能。我们的评估表明，与代表性 GNN 和数据集上的最先进作品相比，Voyager 在 GPU 上实现了平均 3.5 倍的加速。

papers.title: Wave: Offloading Resource Management to SmartNIC Cores.
papers.abstract: SmartNICs are increasingly deployed in datacenters to offload tasks from server CPUs, improving the efficiency and flexibility of datacenter security, networking and storage. Optimizing cloud server efficiency in this way is critically important to ensure that virtually all server resources are available to paying customers. Userspace system software, specifically, decision-making tasks performed by various operating system subsystems, is particularly well suited for execution on mid-tier SmartNIC ARM cores. To this end, we introduce Wave, a framework for offloading userspace system software to processes/agents running on the SmartNIC. Wave uses Linux userspace systems to better align system functionality with SmartNIC capabilities. It also introduces a new host-SmartNIC communication API that enables offloading of even μs-scale system software. To evaluate Wave, we offloaded preexisting userspace system software including kernel thread scheduling, memory management, and an RPC stack to SmartNIC ARM cores, which showed a performance degradation of 1.1%-7.4% in an apples-to-apples comparison with on-host implementations. Wave recovered host resources consumed by on-host system software for memory management (saving 16 host cores), RPCs (saving 8 host cores), and virtual machines (an 11.2% performance improvement). Wave highlights the potential for rethinking system software placement in modern datacenters, unlocking new opportunities for efficiency and scalability.
papers.abstract_zh: SmartNIC 越来越多地部署在数据中心中，以卸载服务器 CPU 的任务，从而提高数据中心安全、网络和存储的效率和灵活性。以这种方式优化云服务器效率对于确保几乎所有服务器资源可供付费客户使用至关重要。用户空间系统软件，特别是由各种操作系统子系统执行的决策任务，特别适合在中层 SmartNIC ARM 内核上执行。为此，我们引入了 Wave，一个用于将用户空间系统软件卸载到 SmartNIC 上运行的进程/代理的框架。 Wave 使用 Linux 用户空间系统来更好地将系统功能与 SmartNIC 功能结合起来。它还引入了新的主机-SmartNIC 通信 API，甚至可以卸载 μs 级的系统软件。为了评估 Wave，我们将现有的用户空间系统软件（包括内核线程调度、内存管理和 RPC 堆栈）卸载到 SmartNIC ARM 内核，与主机上实现进行比较时，性能下降了 1.1%-7.4%。 Wave 恢复了主机系统软件在内存管理（节省 16 个主机核心）、RPC（节省 8 个主机核心）和虚拟机（性能提升 11.2%）方面消耗的主机资源。 Wave 强调了重新思考现代数据中心中系统软件放置的潜力，释放了效率和可扩展性的新机会。

papers.title: Will We Ever have Truly Secure Operating Systems?
papers.abstract:
Half a century after PSOS, the first attempts to prove an operating system (OS) secure, OS faults remain a major threat to computer systems security. A major step forward was the verification of the seL4 microkernel, the first proof of implementation correctness of an OS kernel. Over the next 4 years this proof was extended to the binary code, proofs of security enforcement, and sound and complete worst-case execution-time analysis. The proofs now cover 4 ISAs.

Yet, 15 years later, there is still no provably secure OS. While seL4 has been successfully deployed in defence and civilian security- and safety-critical systems, it is a microkernel that mostly guarantees process isolation without providing the application-oriented services expected from an OS. This not only makes seL4 difficult to deploy, but also means that there is limited assurance that a system built on top is secure in any real sense.

Why has seL4 not been leveraged into a secure OS? In this talk I explore some of the reasons behind this disappointing state of affairs, and what can be done about it. Specifically, I discuss our current work on LionsOS, a new seL4-based OS targeting the embedded/cyberphysical domain, which is easy to use and designed to be verifiable. We aim to achieve our goals by a principled design driven by simplicity, modularity, strict separation of concerns, and highly use-case specific policies, rather than the standard approach of trying to generalise policies.

I also discuss more speculative, early-stage work towards a provably secure, general-purpose OS
papers.abstract_zh:
PSOS 首次尝试证明操作系统 (OS) 安全，半个世纪后，操作系统故障仍然是计算机系统安全的主要威胁。向前迈出的重要一步是对 seL4 微内核的验证，这是操作系统内核实现正确性的第一个证明。在接下来的 4 年里，这个证明扩展到了二进制代码、安全执行证明以及健全且完整的最坏情况执行时间分析。现在的证明涵盖了 4 个 ISA。

然而，15 年后，仍然没有可证明安全的操作系统。虽然 seL4 已成功部署在国防和民用安全关键系统中，但它是一个微内核，主要保证进程隔离，而不提供操作系统期望的面向应用程序的服务。这不仅使得 seL4 难以部署，而且还意味着构建在其之上的系统真正意义上的安全性的保证有限。

为什么 seL4 没有被利用到安全操作系统中？在这次演讲中，我探讨了这种令人失望的事态背后的一些原因，以及对此可以采取的措施。具体来说，我讨论了我们目前在 LionsOS 上的工作，这是一种基于 seL4 的新操作系统，针对嵌入式/网络物理领域，它易于使用且设计可验证。我们的目标是通过由简单性、模块化、严格的关注点分离和高度用例特定的策略驱动的原则性设计来实现我们的目标，而不是试图概括策略的标准方法。

我还讨论了更多推测性的早期工作，以实现可证明安全的通用操作系统

papers.title: ZRAID: Leveraging Zone Random Write Area (ZRWA) for Alleviating Partial Parity Tax in ZNS RAID.
papers.abstract:
The Zoned Namespace (ZNS) SSD is an innovative technology that aims to mitigate the block interface tax associated with conventional SSDs. However, constructing a RAID system using ZNS SSDs presents a significant challenge in managing partial parity for incomplete stripes. Previous research permanently logs partial parity in a limited number of reserved zones, which not only creates bottlenecks in throughput but also exacerbates write amplification, thereby reducing the device's lifetime. We refer to these inefficiencies as the partial parity tax.

In this paper, we present ZRAID, a software ZNS RAID layer that leverages the newly added Zone Random Write Area (ZRWA) feature in the ZNS Command Set, to alleviate partial parity tax. ZRWA enables in-place updates within a confined area near the write pointer. ZRAID temporarily stores partial parity within the ZRWA of data zones. Thus, partial parity writes are distributed across multiple data zones, effectively eliminating throughput bottlenecks. Furthermore, any expired partial parity in the ZRWA is overwritten by subsequent data, avoiding unnecessary flash writes. With the introduction of ZRWA, ZRAID can leverage general schedulers, overcoming the queue depth limitations of ZNS-compatible schedulers. Our evaluation with actual ZNS SSDs demonstrates a significant improvement in write throughput: up to 34.7% in the fio microbenchmark, and an average of 14.5% in db_bench on RocksDB, along with up to a 1.6x reduction in flash write amplification.
papers.abstract_zh:
分区命名空间 (ZNS) SSD 是一项创新技术，旨在减轻与传统 SSD 相关的块接口税。然而，使用 ZNS SSD 构建 RAID 系统在管理不完整条带的部分奇偶校验方面提出了重大挑战。先前的研究将部分奇偶校验永久记录在有限数量的保留区域中，这不仅会造成吞吐量瓶颈，还会加剧写入放大，从而缩短设备的使用寿命。我们将这些低效率称为部分平价税。

在本文中，我们提出了 ZRAID，这是一种软件 ZNS RAID 层，它利用 ZNS 命令集中新添加的区域随机写入区域 (ZRWA) 功能来减轻部分奇偶校验税。 ZRWA 支持在写指针附近的有限区域内进行就地更新。 ZRAID 临时存储数据区域 ZRWA 内的部分奇偶校验。因此，部分奇偶校验写入分布在多个数据区域，有效消除吞吐量瓶颈。此外，ZRWA 中任何过期的部分奇偶校验都会被后续数据覆盖，从而避免不必要的闪存写入。随着 ZRWA 的引入，ZRAID 可以利用通用调度程序，克服 ZNS 兼容调度程序的队列深度限制。我们对实际 ZNS SSD 的评估表明写入吞吐量显着提高：fio 微基准测试中高达 34.7%，RocksDB 上的 db_bench 平均提高 14.5%，闪存写入放大降低高达 1.6 倍。

papers.title: λ-trim: Optimizing Function Initialization in Serverless Applications With Cost-driven Debloating.
papers.abstract:
In this paper, we focus on an often-overlooked component of serverless application cold starts: monetary costs and Function Initialization. Traditionally considered the user's responsibility, Function Initialization is billable and accounts for more than 50% of the monetary cost associated with cold starts in real-world machine-learning applications.

We introduce λ-trim a system that optimizes Python serverless applications by eliminating redundant code while maintaining correctness. To maximize cost savings, λ-trim leverages the typical serverless pricing model to prioritize modules that significantly impact latency and memory usage. λ-trim features an automated pipeline comprising a static analyzer, a profiler specialized for the serverless pricing model, and a debloater. The optimized application can be directly deployed on serverless platforms, leading to substantial reductions in both latency and cost for cold starts.
papers.abstract_zh:
在本文中，我们重点关注无服务器应用程序冷启动中经常被忽视的组成部分：货币成本和函数初始化。传统上，函数初始化被认为是用户的责任，它是可计费的，并且占实际机器学习应用程序中与冷启动相关的货币成本的 50% 以上。

我们引入了 λ-trim 系统，该系统通过消除冗余代码同时保持正确性来优化 Python 无服务器应用程序。为了最大限度地节省成本，λ-trim 利用典型的无服务器定价模型来优先考虑显着影响延迟和内存使用的模块。 λ-trim 具有一个自动化管道，包括静态分析器、专门用于无服务器定价模型的分析器和 debloater。优化后的应用程序可以直接部署在无服务器平台上，从而大幅降低冷启动的延迟和成本。

papers.title: 8-bit Transformer Inference and Fine-tuning for Edge Accelerators.
papers.abstract:
Transformer models achieve state-of-the-art accuracy on natural language processing (NLP) and vision tasks, but demand significant computation and memory resources, which makes it difficult to perform inference and training (fine-tuning) on edge accelerators. Quantization to lower precision data types is a promising way to reduce computation and memory resources. Prior work has employed 8-bit integer (int8) quantization for Transformer inference, but int8 lacks the precision and range required for training. 8-bit floating-point (FP8) quantization has been used for Transformer training, but prior work only quantizes the inputs to matrix multiplications and leaves the rest of the operations in high precision.

This work conducts an in-depth analysis of Transformer inference and fine-tuning at the edge using two 8-bit floating-point data types: FP8 and 8-bit posit (Posit8). Unlike FP8, posit has variable length exponent and fraction fields, leading to higher precision for values around 1, making it well suited for storing Transformer weights and activations. As opposed to prior work, we evaluate the impact of quantizing all operations in both the forward and backward passes, going beyond just matrix multiplications. Specifically, our work makes the following contributions: (1) We perform Transformer inference in FP8 and Posit8, achieving less than 1% accuracy loss compared to BFloat16 through operation fusion, without the need for scaling factors. (2) We perform Transformer fine-tuning in 8 bits by adapting low-rank adaptation (LoRA) to Posit8 and FP8, enabling 8-bit GEMM operations with increased multiply-accumulate efficiency and reduced memory accesses. (3) We design an area- and power-efficient posit softmax, which employs bitwise operations to approximate the exponential and reciprocal functions. The resulting vector unit in the Posit8 accelerator, that performs both softmax computation and other element-wise operations in Transformers, is on average 33% smaller and consumes 35% less power than the vector unit in the FP8 accelerator, while maintaining the same level of accuracy. Our work demonstrates that both Posit8 and FP8 can achieve inference and fine-tuning accuracy comparable to BFloat16, while reducing accelerator's area by 30% and 34%, and power consumption by 26% and 32%, respectively.
papers.abstract_zh:
Transformer 模型在自然语言处理 (NLP) 和视觉任务上实现了最先进的准确性，但需要大量的计算和内存资源，这使得在边缘加速器上执行推理和训练（微调）变得困难。量化为较低精度的数据类型是减少计算和内存资源的一种有前途的方法。之前的工作已采用 8 位整数 (int8) 量化进行 Transformer 推理，但 int8 缺乏训练所需的精度和范围。 8 位浮点 (FP8) 量化已用于 Transformer 训练，但之前的工作仅量化矩阵乘法的输入，而使其余运算保持高精度。

这项工作使用两种 8 位浮点数据类型：FP8 和 8 位 posit（Posit8），对 Transformer 的推理和边缘微调进行了深入分析。与 FP8 不同，posit 具有可变长度的指数和分数字段，导致 1 左右的值具有更高的精度，使其非常适合存储 Transformer 权重和激活。与之前的工作相反，我们评估了量化前向和后向传递中所有操作的影响，而不仅仅是矩阵乘法。具体来说，我们的工作做出了以下贡献：（1）我们在 FP8 和 Posit8 中进行 Transformer 推理，通过运算融合实现了与 BFloat16 相比不到 1% 的精度损失，且不需要缩放因子。 （2）我们通过将低秩自适应（LoRA）适应Posit8和FP8来进行8位Transformer微调，从而实现8位GEMM运算，提高乘法累加效率并减少内存访问。 (3) 我们设计了一个面积和功率高效的 p​​osit softmax，它采用按位运算来逼近指数函数和倒数函数。 Posit8 加速器中生成的向量单元（在 Transformers 中执行 softmax 计算和其他逐元素运算）比 FP8 加速器中的向量单元平均小 33%，功耗降低 35%，同时保持相同的精度水平。我们的工作表明，Posit8 和 FP8 都可以实现与 BFloat16 相当的推理和微调精度，同时将加速器面积分别减少 30% 和 34%，功耗分别减少 26% 和 32%。

papers.title: A Fault-Tolerant Million Qubit-Scale Distributed Quantum Computer.
papers.abstract:
A million qubit-scale quantum computer is essential to realize the quantum supremacy. Modern large-scale quantum computers integrate multiple quantum computers located in dilution refrigerators (DR) to overcome each DR's unscaling cooling budget. However, a large-scale multi-DR quantum computer introduces its unique challenges (i.e., slow and erroneous inter-DR entanglement, increased qubit scale), and they make the baseline error handling mechanism ineffective by increasing the number of gate operations and the inter-DR communication latency to decode and correct errors. Without resolving these challenges, it is impossible to realize a fault-tolerant large-scale multi-DR quantum computer.

In this paper, we propose a million qubit-scale distributed quantum computer which uses a novel error handling mechanism enabling fault-tolerant multi-DR quantum computing. First, we apply a low-overhead multi-DR error syndrome measurement (ESM) sequence to reduce both the number of gate operations and the error rate. Second, we apply a scalable multi-DR error decoding unit (EDU) architecture to maximize both the decoding speed and accuracy. Our multi-DR error handling SW-HW co-design improves the ESM latency, ESM errors, EDU latency, and EDU accuracy by 3.7 times, 2.4 times, 685 times, and 6.1 · 10 10 times, respectively.

With our scheme applied to assumed voltage-scaled CMOS and mature ERSFQ technologies, we successfully build a fault-tolerant million qubit-scale quantum computer.
papers.abstract_zh:
要实现量子霸权，百万量子比特级的量子计算机是必不可少的。现代大型量子计算机集成了位于稀释冰箱 (DR) 中的多台量子计算机，以克服每个 DR 的非缩放冷却预算。然而，大规模多 DR 量子计算机带来了其独特的挑战（即缓慢且错误的 DR 间纠缠、增加的量子位规模），并且通过增加门操作数量和解码和纠正错误的 DR 间通信延迟，使基线错误处理机制变得无效。如果不解决这些挑战，就不可能实现容错的大规模多DR量子计算机。

在本文中，我们提出了百万量子位规模的分布式量子计算机，它使用一种新颖的错误处理机制来实现容错多DR量子计算。首先，我们应用低开销的多 DR 错误综合症测量 (ESM) 序列来减少门操作的数量和错误率。其次，我们应用可扩展的多 DR 错误解码单元 (EDU) 架构来最大限度地提高解码速度和准确性。我们的多DR错误处理软硬件协同设计将ESM延迟、ESM错误、EDU延迟和EDU精度分别提高了3.7倍、2.4倍、685倍和6.1·10 10倍。

通过将我们的方案应用于假设的电压缩放 CMOS 和成熟的 ERSFQ 技术，我们成功构建了容错的百万量子位级量子计算机。

papers.title: A Journey of a 1, 000 Kernels Begins with a Single Step: A Retrospective of Deep Learning on GPUs.
papers.abstract: We are in age of AI, with rapidly changing algorithms and a somewhat synergistic change in hardware. MLPerf is a recent benchmark suite that serves as a way to compare and evaluate hardware. However it has several drawbacks - it is dominated by CNNs and does a poor job of capturing the diversity of AI use cases, and only represents a sliver of production AI use cases. This paper performs a longitudinal study of state-of-art AI applications spanning vision, physical simulation, vision synthesis, language and speech processing, and tabular data processing, across three generations of hardware to understand how the AI revolution has panned out. We call this collection of applications and execution scaffolding the CaSiO suite. The paper reports on data gathered at the framework level, device API level, and hardware and microarchitecture level. The paper provides insights on the hardware-software revolution with pointers to future trends.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们正处于人工智能时代，算法快速变化，硬件也发生一定程度的协同变化。 MLPerf 是最新的基准测试套件，可用作比较和评估硬件的方法。然而它有几个缺点——它以 CNN 为主，在捕获人工智能用例的多样性方面表现不佳，并且只代表了生产人工智能用例的一小部分。本文对跨三代硬件的视觉、物理模拟、视觉合成、语言和语音处理以及表格数据处理等最先进的人工智能应用进行了纵向研究，以了解人工智能革命是如何取得成功的。我们将这个应用程序和执行脚手架的集合称为 CaSiO 套件。该论文报告了在框架级别、设备 API 级别以及硬件和微架构级别收集的数据。该论文提供了对硬件软件革命的见解，并指出了未来的趋势。

papers.title: A Midsummer Night's Tree: Efficient and High Performance Secure SCM.
papers.abstract: Secure memory is a highly desirable property to prevent memory corruption-based attacks. The emergence of nonvolatile, storage class memory (SCM) devices presents new challenges for secure memory. Metadata for integrity verification, organized in a Bonsai Merkle Tree (BMT), is cached on-chip in volatile caches, and may be lost on a power failure. As a consequence, care is required to ensure that metadata updates are always propagated into SCM. To optimize metadata updates, state-of-the-art approaches propose lazy update crash consistent metadata schemes. However, few consider the implications of their optimizations on on-chip area, which leads to inefficient utilization of scarce on-chip space. In this paper, we propose A Midsummer Night's Tree (AMNT), a novel "tree within a tree" approach to provide crash consistent integrity with low run-time overhead while limiting on-chip area for security metadata. Our approach offloads the potential hardware complexity of our technique to software to keep area overheads low. Our proposed mechanism results in significant improvements (a 41% reduction in execution overhead on average versus the state-of-the-art) for in-memory storage applications while significantly reducing the required on-chip area to implement our protocol.
papers.abstract_zh: 安全内存是防止基于内存损坏的攻击的非常理想的属性。非易失性存储级内存 (SCM) 设备的出现给安全内存带来了新的挑战。用于完整性验证的元数据以 Bonsai Merkle Tree (BMT) 的形式组织，缓存在片上的易失性缓存中，并且可能会在电源故障时丢失。因此，需要注意确保元数据更新始终传播到 SCM 中。为了优化元数据更新，最先进的方法提出了延迟更新崩溃一致的元数据方案。然而，很少有人考虑其优化对片上面积的影响，从而导致稀缺的片上空间利用效率低下。在本文中，我们提出了仲夏夜之树（AMNT），这是一种新颖的“树中之树”方法，以低运行时开销提供崩溃一致的完整性，同时限制安全元数据的片上区域。我们的方法将我们技术的潜在硬件复杂性转移到软件上，以保持较低的面积开销。我们提出的机制为内存存储应用带来了显着的改进（与最先进的技术相比，执行开销平均减少了 41%），同时显着减少了实现我们的协议所需的片上面积。

papers.title: A Quantitative Analysis and Guidelines of Data Streaming Accelerator in Modern Intel Xeon Scalable Processors.
papers.abstract: As semiconductor power density is no longer constant with the technology process scaling down, we need different solutions if we are to continue scaling application performance. To this end, modern CPUs are integrating capable data accelerators on the chip, aiming to improve performance and efficiency for a wide range of applications and usages. One such accelerator is the Intel® Data Streaming Accelerator (DSA) introduced since Intel® 4 th Generation Xeon® Scalable CPUs (Sapphire Rapids). DSA targets data movement operations in memory that are common sources of overhead in datacenter workloads and infrastructure. In addition, it supports a wider range of operations on streaming data, such as CRC32 calculations, computation of deltas between data buffers, and data integrity field (DIF) operations. This paper aims to introduce the latest features supported by DSA, dive deep into its versatility, and analyze its throughput benefits through a comprehensive evaluation with both microbenchmarks and real use cases. Along with the analysis of its characteristics and the rich software ecosystem of DSA, we summarize several insights and guidelines for the programmer to make the most out of DSA, and use an in-depth case study of DPDK Vhost to demonstrate how these guidelines benefit a real application.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着技术工艺缩小，半导体功率密度不再恒定，如果我们要继续扩展应用性能，我们需要不同的解决方案。为此，现代 CPU 在芯片上集成了功能强大的数据加速器，旨在提高各种应用和用途的性能和效率。其中一种加速器是自英特尔® 第四代至强® 可扩展 CPU (Sapphire Rapids) 以来推出的英特尔® 数据流加速器 (DSA)。 DSA 的目标是内存中的数据移动操作，这些操作是数据中心工作负载和基础设施中常见的开销来源。此外，它还支持对流数据进行更广泛的操作，例如CRC32计算、数据缓冲区之间的增量计算以及数据完整性字段（DIF）操作。本文旨在介绍 DSA 支持的最新功能，深入探讨其多功能性，并通过微基准测试和实际用例的综合评估来分析其吞吐量优势。在分析 DSA 的特点和丰富的软件生态系统的同时，我们总结了一些见解和指南，帮助程序员充分利用 DSA，并通过 DPDK Vhost 的深入案例研究来展示这些指南如何使实际应用受益。

papers.title: A shared compilation stack for distributed-memory parallelism in stencil DSLs.
papers.abstract: Domain Specific Languages (DSLs) increase programmer productivity and provide high performance. Their targeted abstractions allow scientists to express problems at a high level, providing rich details that optimizing compilers can exploit to target current- and next-generation supercomputers. The convenience and performance of DSLs come with significant development and maintenance costs. The siloed design of DSL compilers and the resulting inability to benefit from shared infrastructure cause uncertainties around longevity and the adoption of DSLs at scale. By tailoring the broadly-adopted MLIR compiler framework to HPC, we bring the same synergies that the machine learning community already exploits across their DSLs (e.g. Tensorflow, PyTorch) to the finite-difference stencil HPC community. We introduce new HPC-specific abstractions for message passing targeting distributed stencil computations. We demonstrate the sharing of common components across three distinct HPC stencil-DSL compilers: Devito, PSyclone, and the Open Earth Compiler, showing that our framework generates high-performance executables based upon a shared compiler ecosystem.
papers.abstract_zh: 领域特定语言 (DSL) 提高程序员的工作效率并提供高性能。他们有针对性的抽象使科学家能够在高层次上表达问题，提供丰富的细节，优化编译器可以利用这些细节来瞄准当前和下一代超级计算机。 DSL 的便利性和性能伴随着巨大的开发和维护成本。 DSL 编译器的孤立设计以及由此导致的无法从共享基础设施中受益，导致了 DSL 的寿命和大规模采用方面的不确定性。通过针对 HPC 定制广泛采用的 MLIR 编译器框架，我们将机器学习社区已经在其 DSL（例如 Tensorflow、PyTorch）中利用的协同效应引入有限差分模板 HPC 社区。我们引入了新的 HPC 特定抽象，用于针对分布式模板计算的消息传递。我们演示了三个不同 HPC stencil-DSL 编译器（Devito、PSyclone 和 Open Earth Compiler）之间的通用组件的共享，表明我们的框架基于共享编译器生态系统生成高性能可执行文件。

papers.title: A Software Caching Runtime for Embedded NVRAM Systems.
papers.abstract:
Increasingly sophisticated low-power microcontrollers are at the heart of millions of IoT and edge computing deployments, with developers pushing large-scale data collection, processing, and inference to end nodes. Advanced workloads on resource-constrained systems depend on emerging technologies to meet performance and lifetime demands. High-performance Non-Volatile RAMs (NVRAMs) are one such technology enabling a new class of systems previously made impossible by memory limitations, including ultra-low-power designs using program state non-volatility and sensing systems storing and processing large blocks of data.

Unfortunately, existing NVRAM significantly underperforms SRAM's access latency/energy cost and flash's read performance---condemning systems dependent on NVRAM to pay a steep energy and time penalty for software execution. We observe that this performance penalty stems predominately from instruction fetches into NVRAM, which represent >75% of memory accesses in typical embedded software. To eliminate this performance bottleneck, we propose SwapRAM , a new operating model for NVRAM-based platforms which repurposes underutilized SRAM as an instruction cache, maximizing the proportion of accesses directed towards higher-performance SRAM. SwapRAM consists of a set of compile-time code transformations and a runtime management system that transparently and dynamically copies code into SRAM throughout execution, with an extensible logic to delay eviction of hot code. Across nine embedded benchmarks running on a real FRAM platform, SwapRAM 's software-based design increases execution speed by up to 46% (average 26%) and reduces energy consumption by up to 36% (average 24%) compared to a baseline system using the existing hardware cache.
papers.abstract_zh:
日益复杂的低功耗微控制器是数以百万计的物联网和边缘计算部署的核心，开发人员将大规模数据收集、处理和推理推向终端节点。资源受限系统上的高级工作负载依赖于新兴技术来满足性能和生命周期需求。高性能非易失性 RAM (NVRAM) 就是这样一种技术，可实现以前因内存限制而无法实现的新型系统，包括使用程序状态非易失性的超低功耗设计以及存储和处理大数据块的传感系统。

不幸的是，现有 NVRAM 的访问延迟/能源成本和闪存的读取性能明显低于 SRAM，这使得依赖 NVRAM 的系统必须为软件执行付出高昂的能源和时间代价。我们观察到，这种性能损失主要源于 NVRAM 中的指令提取，这代表了典型嵌入式软件中超过 75% 的内存访问。为了消除这一性能瓶颈，我们提出了 SwapRAM，这是一种针对基于 NVRAM 的平台的新操作模型，它将未充分利用的 SRAM 重新用作指令缓存，从而最大限度地提高针对高性能 SRAM 的访问比例。 SwapRAM 由一组编译时代码转换和一个运行时管理系统组成，该系统在整个执行过程中透明且动态地将代码复制到 SRAM 中，并具有可扩展逻辑来延迟热代码的驱逐。在真实 FRAM 平台上运行的九个嵌入式基准测试中，与使用现有硬件缓存的基准系统相比，SwapRAM 基于软件的设计将执行速度提高了高达 46%（平均 26%），并将能耗降低了高达 36%（平均 24%）。

papers.title: Accelerating Multi-Scalar Multiplication for Efficient Zero Knowledge Proofs with Multi-GPU Systems.
papers.abstract: Zero-knowledge proof is a cryptographic primitive that allows for the validation of statements without disclosing any sensitive information, foundational in applications like verifiable outsourcing and digital currency. However, the extensive proof generation time limits its widespread adoption. Even with GPU acceleration, proof generation can still take minutes, with Multi-Scalar Multiplication (MSM) accounting for about 78.2% of the workload. To address this, we present DistMSM , a novel MSM algorithm tailored for distributed multi-GPU systems. At the algorithmic level, DistMSM adapts Pippenger's algorithm for multi-GPU setups, effectively identifying and addressing bottlenecks that emerge during scaling. At the GPU kernel level, DistMSM introduces an elliptic curve arithmetic kernel tailored for contemporary GPU architectures. It optimizes register pressure with two innovative techniques and leverages tensor cores for specific big integer multiplications. Compared to state-of-the-art MSM implementations, DistMSM offers an average 6.39× speedup across various elliptic curves and GPU counts. An MSM task that previously took seconds on a single GPU can now be completed in mere tens of milliseconds. It showcases the substantial potential and efficiency of distributed multi-GPU systems in ZKP acceleration.
papers.abstract_zh: 零知识证明是一种加密原语，允许在不泄露任何敏感信息的情况下验证语句，是可验证外包和数字货币等应用的基础。然而，较长的证明生成时间限制了其广泛采用。即使使用 GPU 加速，证明生成仍然需要几分钟的时间，其中多标量乘法 (MSM) 约占工作负载的 78.2%。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 DistMSM，这是一种专为分布式多 GPU 系统量身定制的新型 MSM 算法。在算法层面，DistMSM 采用 Pippenger 的算法来实现多 GPU 设置，有效识别和解决扩展过程中出现的瓶颈。在GPU内核层面，DistMSM引入了专为当代GPU架构量身定制的椭圆曲线算术内核。它通过两种创新技术优化寄存器压力，并利用张量核心进行特定的大整数乘法。与最先进的 MSM 实现相比，DistMSM 在各种椭圆曲线和 GPU 数量上提供平均 6.39 倍的加速。以前在单个 GPU 上需要几秒钟的 MSM 任务现在只需几十毫秒即可完成。它展示了分布式多 GPU 系统在 ZKP 加速方面的巨大潜力和效率。

papers.title: ACES: Accelerating Sparse Matrix Multiplication with Adaptive Execution Flow and Concurrency-Aware Cache Optimizations.
papers.abstract: Sparse matrix-matrix multiplication (SpMM) is a critical computational kernel in numerous scientific and machine learning applications. SpMM involves massive irregular memory accesses and poses great challenges to conventional cache-based computer architectures. Recently dedicated SpMM accelerators have been proposed to enhance SpMM performance. However, current SpMM accelerators still face challenges in adapting to varied sparse patterns, fully exploiting inherent parallelism, and optimizing cache performance. To address these issues, we introduce ACES, a novel SpMM accelerator in this study. First, ACES features an adaptive execution flow that dynamically adjusts to diverse sparse patterns. The adaptive execution flow balances parallel computing efficiency and data reuse. Second, ACES incorporates locality-concurrency co-optimizations within the global cache. ACES utilizes a concurrency-aware cache management policy, which considers data locality and concurrency for optimal replacement decisions. Additionally, the integration of a non-blocking buffer with the global cache enhances concurrency and reduces computational stalls. Third, the hardware architecture of ACES is designed to integrate all innovations. The architecture ensures efficient support across the adaptive execution flow, advanced cache optimizations, and fine-grained parallel processing. Our performance evaluation demonstrates that ACES significantly outperforms existing solutions, providing a 2.1× speedup and marking a substantial advancement in SpMM acceleration.
papers.abstract_zh: 稀疏矩阵-矩阵乘法 (SpMM) 是众多科学和机器学习应用中的关键计算内核。 SpMM涉及大量不规则的内存访问，对传统的基于缓存的计算机体系结构提出了巨大的挑战。最近提出了专用 SpMM 加速器来增强 SpMM 性能。然而，当前的SpMM加速器在适应不同的稀疏模式、充分利用固有的并行性和优化缓存性能方面仍然面临挑战。为了解决这些问题，我们在本研究中引入了 ACES，一种新型 SpMM 加速器。首先，ACES 具有自适应执行流程，可以动态调整以适应不同的稀疏模式。自适应执行流程平衡了并行计算效率和数据重用。其次，ACES 在全局缓存中纳入了局部性并发协同优化。 ACES 采用并发感知缓存管理策略，该策略考虑数据局部性和并发性以做出最佳替换决策。此外，非阻塞缓冲区与全局缓存的集成增强了并发性并减少了计算停顿。第三，ACES的硬件架构旨在集成所有创新。该架构确保对自适应执行流、高级缓存优化和细粒度并行处理的有效支持。我们的性能评估表明，ACES 的性能显着优于现有解决方案，提供 2.1 倍的加速，标志着 SpMM 加速的重大进步。

papers.title: Achieving Near-Zero Read Retry for 3D NAND Flash Memory.
papers.abstract: As the flash-based storage devices age with program/erase (P/E) cycles, they require an increasing number of read retries for error correction, which in turn deteriorates their read performance. The design of read-retry methods is critical to flash read performance. Current flash chips embed pre-defined read retry tables (RRT) for retry, but these tables fail to consider the read granularity and error behaviors. We characterize different types of real flash chips, based on which we further develop models for the correlation among the optimal read offsets of read voltages required for reading each page. By leveraging characterization observations and the models, we propose a methodology to generate a tailored RRT for each flash model. We introduce a dynamic read retry procedure to pick up proper read voltages from the table, followed by a proximity-search method for fine-tuning the read offsets. Experiments on real flash chips show that the proposed methodology can achieve near-zero retries. It reduces the average number of read retries to below 0.003 for data with high retention time at 8K P/E cycles, whereas the state-of-the-art approaches incur over 3 read retries on average once the flash is aged to 3K P/E cycles.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着基于闪存的存储设备随着编程/擦除（P/E）周期的老化，它们需要越来越多的读取重试次数来纠正错误，这反过来又降低了它们的读取性能。读重试方法的设计对于闪存读取性能至关重要。当前的闪存芯片嵌入了预定义的读重试表（RRT）用于重试，但这些表未能考虑读粒度和错误行为。我们表征了不同类型的真实闪存芯片，在此基础上我们进一步开发了读取每个页面所需的读取电压的最佳读取偏移之间的相关性模型。通过利用特征观察和模型，我们提出了一种为每个闪存模型生成定制 RRT 的方法。我们引入了动态读取重试过程，以从表中获取适当的读取电压，然后采用邻近搜索方法来微调读取偏移。在真实闪存芯片上的实验表明，所提出的方法可以实现接近零的重试。对于 8K P/E 周期具有高保留时间的数据，它将平均读取重试次数减少到 0.003 以下，而一旦闪存老化到 3K P/E 周期，最先进的方法平均会产生超过 3 次读取重试。

papers.title: AdaPipe: Optimizing Pipeline Parallelism with Adaptive Recomputation and Partitioning.
papers.abstract:
Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated powerful capabilities, requiring huge memory with their increasing sizes and sequence lengths, thus demanding larger parallel systems. The broadly adopted pipeline parallelism introduces even heavier and unbalanced memory consumption. Recomputation is a widely employed technique to mitigate the problem but introduces extra computation overhead.

This paper proposes AdaPipe, which aims to find the optimized recomputation and pipeline stage partitioning strategy. AdaPipe employs adaptive recomputation to maximize memory utilization and reduce the computation cost of each pipeline stage. A flexible stage partitioning algorithm is also adopted to balance the computation between different stages. We evaluate AdaPipe by training two representative models, GPT-3 (175B) and Llama 2 (70B), achieving up to 1.32× and 1.22× speedup on clusters with NVIDIA GPUs and Ascend NPUs respectively.
papers.abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLM）已展现出强大的功能，随着其大小和序列长度的增加，需要巨大的内存，从而需要更大的并行系统。广泛采用的管道并行性引入了更重且不平衡的内存消耗。重新计算是一种广泛采用的技术来缓解该问题，但会带来额外的计算开销。

本文提出AdaPipe，旨在寻找优化的重新计算和管道阶段划分策略。 AdaPipe 采用自适应重新计算来最大限度地提高内存利用率并降低每个管道阶段的计算成本。还采用灵活的阶段划分算法来平衡不同阶段之间的计算。我们通过训练两个代表性模型 GPT-3 (175B) 和 Llama 2 (70B) 来评估 AdaPipe，在使用 NVIDIA GPU 和 Ascend NPU 的集群上分别实现了高达 1.32 倍和 1.22 倍的加速。

papers.title: AERO: Adaptive Erase Operation for Improving Lifetime and Performance of Modern NAND Flash-Based SSDs.
papers.abstract: This work investigates a new erase scheme in NAND flash memory to improve the lifetime and performance of modern solid-state drives (SSDs). In NAND flash memory, an erase operation applies a high voltage (e.g., > 20 V) to flash cells for a long time (e.g., > 3.5 ms), which degrades cell endurance and potentially delays user I/O requests. While a large body of prior work has proposed various techniques to mitigate the negative impact of erase operations, no work has yet investigated how erase latency should be set to fully exploit the potential of NAND flash memory; most existing techniques use a fixed latency for every erase operation which is set to cover the worst-case operating conditions. To address this, we propose Aero (Adaptive ERase Operation), a new erase scheme that dynamically adjusts erase latency to be just long enough for reliably erasing target cells, depending on the cells' current erase characteristics. Aero accurately predicts such near-optimal erase latency based on the number of fail bits during an erase operation. To maximize its benefits, we further optimize Aero in two aspects. First, at the beginning of an erase operation, Aero attempts to erase the cells for a short time (e.g., 1 ms), which enables Aero to always obtain the number of fail bits necessary to accurately predict the near-optimal erase latency. Second, Aero aggressively yet safely reduces erase latency by leveraging a large reliability margin present in modern SSDs. We demonstrate the feasibility and reliability of Aero using 160 real 3D NAND flash chips, showing that it enhances SSD lifetime over the conventional erase scheme by 43% without change to existing NAND flash chips. Our system-level evaluation using eleven real-world workloads shows that an AERO-enabled SSD reduces read tail latency by 34% on average over a state-of-the-art technique.
papers.abstract_zh: 这项工作研究了 NAND 闪存中的新擦除方案，以提高现代固态硬盘 (SSD) 的使用寿命和性能。在 NAND 闪存中，擦除操作会长时间（例如，> 3.5 ms）向闪存单元施加高电压（例如，> 20 V），这会降低单元耐用性并可能延迟用户 I/O 请求。虽然大量先前的工作提出了各种技术来减轻擦除操作的负面影响，但尚未有工作研究如何设置擦除延迟以充分发挥 NAND 闪存的潜力；大多数现有技术对每个擦除操作都使用固定的延迟，该延迟被设置为覆盖最坏情况的操作条件。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 Aero（自适应擦除操作），这是一种新的擦除方案，可以根据单元的当前擦除特性动态调整擦除延迟，使其足够长以可靠地擦除目标单元。 Aero 根据擦除操作期间的失败位数量准确预测这种接近最佳的擦除延迟。为了最大化其效益，我们在两个方面进一步优化Aero。首先，在擦除操作开始时，Aero 尝试在短时间内（例如 1 ms）擦除单元，这使得 Aero 始终能够获得准确预测接近最佳擦除延迟所需的失败位数量。其次，Aero 通过利用现代 SSD 中存在的巨大可靠性裕度，积极而安全地减少擦除延迟。我们使用 160 个真实 3D NAND 闪存芯片演示了 Aero 的可行性和可靠性，表明它在不改变现有 NAND 闪存芯片的情况下，比传统擦除方案将 SSD 寿命提高了 43%。我们使用 11 个实际工作负载进行的系统级评估表明，与最先进的技术相比，支持 AERO 的 SSD 平均将读尾延迟减少了 34%。

papers.title: Amanda: Unified Instrumentation Framework for Deep Neural Networks.
papers.abstract: The success of deep neural networks (DNNs) has sparked efforts to analyze (e.g., tracing) and optimize (e.g., pruning) them. These tasks have specific requirements and ad-hoc implementations in current execution backends like TensorFlow/PyTorch, which require developers to manage fragmented interfaces and adapt their codes to diverse models. In this study, we propose a new framework called Amanda to streamline the development of these tasks. We formalize the implementation of these tasks as neural network instrumentation , which involves introducing instrumentation into the operator level of DNNs. This allows us to abstract DNN analysis and optimization tasks as instrumentation tools on various DNN models. We build Amanda with two levels of APIs to achieve a unified, extensible, and efficient instrumentation design. The user-level API provides a unified operator-grained instrumentation API for different backends. Meanwhile, internally, we design a set of callback-centric APIs for managing and optimizing the execution of original and instrumentation codes in different backends. Through these design principles, the Amanda framework can accommodate a broad spectrum of use cases, such as tracing, profiling, pruning, and quantization, across different backends (e.g., TensorFlow/PyTorch) and execution modes (graph/eager mode). Moreover, our efficient execution management ensures that the performance overhead is typically kept within 5%.
papers.abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 的成功引发了对其进行分析（例如追踪）和优化（例如修剪）的努力。这些任务在当前的执行后端（如 TensorFlow/PyTorch）中具有特定的要求和临时实现，这要求开发人员管理碎片化的接口并使代码适应不同的模型。在这项研究中，我们提出了一个名为 Amanda 的新框架来简化这些任务的开发。我们将这些任务的实现形式化为神经网络仪器，其中涉及将仪器引入 DNN 的操作员级别。这使我们能够将 DNN 分析和优化任务抽象为各种 DNN 模型上的检测工具。我们构建了具有两级 API 的 Amanda，以实现统一、可扩展且高效的仪器设计。用户级API为不同后端提供统一的算子粒度的仪表API。同时，在内部，我们设计了一组以回调为中心的API，用于管理和优化不同后端中原始代码和检测代码的执行。通过这些设计原则，Amanda 框架可以适应广泛的用例，例如跨不同后端（例如 TensorFlow/PyTorch）和执行模式（图形/渴望模式）的跟踪、分析、修剪和量化。此外，我们高效的执行管理可确保性能开销通常保持在 5% 以内。

papers.title: An Encoding Scheme to Enlarge Practical DNA Storage Capacity by Reducing Primer-Payload Collisions.
papers.abstract:
Deoxyribonucleic Acid (DNA), with its ultra-high storage density and long durability, is a promising long-term archival storage medium and is attracting much attention today. A DNA storage system encodes and stores digital data with synthetic DNA sequences and decodes DNA sequences back to digital data via sequencing. Many encoding schemes have been proposed to enlarge DNA storage capacity by increasing DNA encoding density. However, only increasing encoding density is insufficient because enhancing DNA storage capacity is a multifaceted problem.

This paper assumes that random accesses are necessary for practical DNA archival storage. We identify all factors affecting DNA storage capacity under current technologies and systematically investigate the practical DNA storage capacity with several popular encoding schemes. The investigation result shows the collision between primers and DNA payload sequences is a major factor limiting DNA storage capacity. Based on this discovery, we designed a new encoding scheme called Collision Aware Code (CAC) to trade some encoding density for the reduction of primer-payload collisions. Compared with the best result among the five existing encoding schemes, CAC can extricate 120% more primers from collisions and increase the DNA tube capacity from 211.96 GB to 295.11 GB. Besides, we also evaluate CAC's recoverability from DNA storage errors. The result shows CAC is comparable to those of existing encoding schemes.
papers.abstract_zh:
脱氧核糖核酸（DNA）以其超高的存储密度和长久的耐用性，是一种很有前途的长期档案存储介质，如今备受关注。 DNA 存储系统使用合成 DNA 序列编码和存储数字数据，并通过测序将 DNA 序列解码回数字数据。已经提出了许多编码方案来通过增加DNA编码密度来扩大DNA存储容量。然而，仅仅增加编码密度是不够的，因为增强DNA存储容量是一个多方面的问题。

本文假设随机访问对于实际 DNA 档案存储是必要的。我们确定了当前技术下影响 DNA 存储容量的所有因素，并系统地研究了几种流行编码方案的实际 DNA 存储容量。调查结果表明引物和DNA有效负载序列之间的碰撞是限制DNA存储容量的主要因素。基于这一发现，我们设计了一种称为碰撞感知代码（CAC）的新编码方案，以牺牲一些编码密度来减少引物有效负载碰撞。与现有五种编码方案中的最佳结果相比，CAC可以使引物免于碰撞多120%，并将DNA管容量从211.96 GB增加到295.11 GB。此外，我们还评估了 CAC 从 DNA 存储错误中恢复的能力。结果表明CAC与现有的编码方案相当。

papers.title: Atalanta: A Bit is Worth a "Thousand" Tensor Values.
papers.abstract: Atalanta is a lossless , hardware/software co-designed compression technique for the tensors of fixed-point quantized deep neural networks. Atalanta increases effective memory capacity, reduces off-die traffic, and/or helps to achieve the desired performance/energy targets while using smaller off-die memories during inference. Atalanta is architected to deliver nearly identical coding efficiency compared to Arithmetic Coding while avoiding its complexity, overhead, and bandwidth limitations. Indicatively, the Atalanta decoder and encoder units each use less than 50B of internal storage. In hardware, Atalanta is implemented as an assist over any machine learning accelerator transparently compressing/decompressing tensors just before the off-die memory controller. This work shows the performance and energy efficiency of Atalanta when implemented in a 65nm technology node. Atalanta reduces data footprint of weights and activations to 60% and 48% respectively on average over a wide set of 8-bit quantized models and complements a wide range of quantization methods. Integrated with a Tensorcore-based accelerator, Atalanta boosts the speedup and energy efficiency to 1.44× and 1.37×, respectively. Atalanta is effective at compressing the stashed activations during training for fixed-point inference.
papers.abstract_zh: Atalanta 是一种无损、硬件/软件共同设计的压缩技术，用于定点量化深度神经网络的张量。 Atalanta 增加了有效内存容量，减少了芯片外流量，和/或帮助实现所需的性能/能源目标，同时在推理过程中使用更小的芯片外内存。 Atalanta 的架构旨在提供与算术编码几乎相同的编码效率，同时避免其复杂性、开销和带宽限制。值得注意的是，Atalanta 解码器和编码器单元各自使用不到 50B 的内部存储空间。在硬件中，Atalanta 被实现为对任何机器学习加速器的辅助，在片外内存控制器之前透明地压缩/解压缩张量。这项工作展示了 Atalanta 在 65 纳米技术节点中实现时的性能和能效。 Atalanta 在广泛的 8 位量化模型中将权重和激活的数据占用量平均分别减少到 60% 和 48%，并补充了广泛的量化方法。 Atalanta 与基于 Tensorcore 的加速器集成，将加速比和能源效率分别提高到 1.44 倍和 1.37 倍。 Atalanta 在定点推理训练期间可以有效压缩隐藏的激活。

papers.title: AttAcc! Unleashing the Power of PIM for Batched Transformer-based Generative Model Inference.
papers.abstract: The Transformer-based generative model (TbGM), comprising summarization (Sum) and generation (Gen) stages, has demonstrated unprecedented generative performance across a wide range of applications. However, it also demands immense amounts of compute and memory resources. Especially, the Gen stages, consisting of the attention and fully-connected (FC) layers, dominate the overall execution time. Meanwhile, we reveal that the conventional system with GPUs used for TbGM inference cannot efficiently execute the attention layer, even with batching, due to various constraints. To address this inefficiency, we first propose AttAcc, a processing-in-memory (PIM) architecture for efficient execution of the attention layer. Subsequently, for the end-to-end acceleration of TbGM inference, we propose a novel heterogeneous system architecture and optimizations that strategically use xPU and PIM together. It leverages the high memory bandwidth of AttAcc for the attention layer and the powerful compute capability of the conventional system for the FC layer. Lastly, we demonstrate that our GPU-PIM system outperforms the conventional system with the same memory capacity, improving performance and energy efficiency of running a 175B TbGM by up to 2.81× and 2.67×, respectively.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于 Transformer 的生成模型 (TbGM) 包括汇总 (Sum) 和生成 (Gen) 阶段，在广泛的应用中展示了前所未有的生成性能。然而，它也需要大量的计算和内存资源。特别是，由注意力层和全连接（FC）层组成的 Gen 阶段主导了整体执行时间。同时，我们发现，由于各种限制，用于 TbGM 推理的具有 GPU 的传统系统即使使用批处理也无法有效地执行注意力层。为了解决这种低效率问题，我们首先提出 AttAcc，一种内存处理（PIM）架构，用于高效执行注意力层。随后，为了实现 TbGM 推理的端到端加速，我们提出了一种新颖的异构系统架构和优化，策略性地结合使用 xPU 和 PIM。它利用 AttAcc 的高内存带宽作为注意力层，并利用传统系统强大的计算能力作为 FC 层。最后，我们证明我们的 GPU-PIM 系统优于具有相同内存容量的传统系统，将运行 175B TbGM 的性能和能源效率分别提高了 2.81 倍和 2.67 倍。

papers.title: AUDIBLE: A Convolution-Based Resource Allocator for Oversubscribing Burstable Virtual Machines.
papers.abstract: In an effort to increase the utilization of data center resources cloud providers have introduced a new type of virtual machine (VM) offering, called a burstable VM (BVM). Our work is the first to study the characteristics of burstable VMs (based on traces from production systems at a major cloud provider) and resource allocation approaches for BVM workloads. We propose new approaches for BVM resource allocation and use extensive simulations driven by field data to compare them with two baseline approaches used in practice. We find that traditional approaches based on using a fixed oversubscription ratio or based on the Central Limit Theorem do not work well for BVMs: They lead to either low utilization or high server capacity violation rates. Based on the lessons learned from our workload study, we develop a new approach to BVM scheduling, called Audible, using a non-parametric statistical model, which makes the approach light-weight and workload independent, and obviates the need for training machine learning models and for tuning their parameters. We show that Audible achieves high system utilization while being able to enforce stringent requirements on server capacity violations.
papers.abstract_zh: 为了提高数据中心资源的利用率，云提供商引入了一种新型虚拟机 (VM) 产品，称为可突发 VM (BVM)。我们的工作是第一个研究可突发虚拟机的特征（基于主要云提供商的生产系统的跟踪）和 BVM 工作负载的资源分配方法。我们提出了 BVM 资源分配的新方法，并使用由现场数据驱动的广泛模拟来将它们与实践中使用的两种基线方法进行比较。我们发现基于使用固定超额认购率或基于中心极限定理的传统方法对于 BVM 效果不佳：它们会导致利用率低或服务器容量违规率高。根据工作负载研究的经验教训，我们开发了一种新的 BVM 调度方法，称为 Audible，使用非参数统计模型，这使得该方法轻量且与工作负载无关，并且无需训练机器学习模型和调整其参数。我们证明 Audible 实现了高系统利用率，同时能够对服务器容量违规执行严格的要求。

papers.title: Automatic Generation of Vectorizing Compilers for Customizable Digital Signal Processors.
papers.abstract:
Embedded applications extract the best power-performance trade-off from digital signal processors (DSPs) by making extensive use of vectorized execution. Rather than handwriting the many customized kernels these applications use, DSP engineers rely on auto-vectorizing compilers to quickly produce effective code. Building these compilers is a large and error-prone investment, and each new DSP architecture or application-specific ISA customization must repeat this effort to derive a new high-performance compiler.

We present Isaria, a framework for automatically generating vectorizing compilers for DSP architectures. Isaria uses equality saturation to search for vectorized DSP code using a system of rewrite rules. Rather than hand-crafting these rules, Isaria automatically synthesizes sound rewrite rules from an ISA specification, discovers phase structure within these rules that improve compilation performance, and schedules their application at compile time while pruning intermediate states of the search. We use Isaria to generate a compiler for an industrial DSP architecture, and show that the resulting kernels outperform existing DSP libraries by up to 6.9× and are competitive with those generated by expert-built compilers. We also demonstrate how Isaria can speed up exploration of new ISA customizations by automatically generating a high-quality vectorizing compiler.
papers.abstract_zh:
嵌入式应用通过广泛使用矢量化执行，从数字信号处理器 (DSP) 中获取最佳功耗性能权衡。 DSP 工程师依靠自动矢量化编译器来快速生成有效的代码，而不是手写这些应用程序使用的许多定制内核。构建这些编译器是一项巨大且容易出错的投资，每个新的 DSP 架构或特定于应用程序的 ISA 定制都必须重复这项工作才能获得新的高性能编译器。

我们提出了 Isaria，一个为 DSP 架构自动生成矢量化编译器的框架。 Isaria 使用等式饱和度通过重写规则系统来搜索矢量化 DSP 代码。 Isaria 不是手工制定这些规则，而是自动从 ISA 规范合成健全的重写规则，发现这些规则中的阶段结构以提高编译性能，并在编译时安排其应用程序，同时修剪搜索的中间状态。我们使用 Isaria 为工业 DSP 架构生成编译器，并表明生成的内核比现有 DSP 库性能高出 6.9 倍，并且与专家构建的编译器生成的内核相比具有竞争力。我们还演示了 Isaria 如何通过自动生成高质量的矢量化编译器来加快对新 ISA 自定义的探索。

papers.title: Avoiding Instruction-Centric Microarchitectural Timing Channels Via Binary-Code Transformations.
papers.abstract:
With the end of Moore's Law-based scaling, novel microarchitectural optimizations are being patented, researched, and implemented at an increasing rate. Previous research has examined recently published patents and papers and demonstrated ways these upcoming optimizations present new security risks via novel side channels. As these side channels are introduced by microarchitectural optimization, they are not generically solvable in source code.

In this paper, we build program analysis and transformation tools for automatically mitigating the security risks introduced by future instruction-centric microarchitectural optimizations. We focus on two classes of optimizations that are not yet deployed: silent stores and computation simplification. Silent stores are known to leak secret data being written to memory by dropping in-flight stores that will have no effect. Computation simplification is known to leak operands to arithmetic instructions by shortcutting trivial computations at execution time. This presents problems that classical constant-time techniques cannot handle: register spills, address calculations, and the micro-ops of complex instructions are all potentially leaky. To address these problems, we design, implement, and evaluate a process and tool, cio, for detecting and mitigating these types of side channels in cryptographic code. cio is a backstop, providing verified mitigation for novel microarchitectural side-channels when more specialized and efficient hardware or software tools, such as microcode patches, are not yet available.
papers.abstract_zh:
随着基于摩尔定律的缩放的结束，新型微架构优化正在以越来越快的速度获得专利、研究和实施。先前的研究审查了最近发表的专利和论文，并展示了这些即将到来的优化如何通过新颖的侧通道带来新的安全风险。由于这些侧通道是通过微架构优化引入的，因此它们通常不能在源代码中解决。

在本文中，我们构建了程序分析和转换工具，用于自动减轻未来以指令为中心的微架构优化带来的安全风险。我们重点关注两类尚未部署的优化：静默存储和计算简化。众所周知，静默存储会通过删除无效的正在进行的存储来泄漏正在写入内存的秘密数据。众所周知，计算简化会通过在执行时简化琐碎的计算来将操作数泄漏给算术指令。这带来了经典恒定时间技术无法处理的问题：寄存器溢出、地址计算和复杂指令的微操作都可能存在泄漏。为了解决这些问题，我们设计、实现和评估了一个流程和工具 cio，用于检测和减轻密码代码中的这些类型的侧信道。 cio 是一种后备方案，当尚未提供更专业、更高效的硬件或软件工具（例如微代码补丁）时，它可以为新颖的微架构侧通道提供经过验证的缓解措施。

papers.title: AWS Trainium: The Journey for Designing and Optimization Full Stack ML Hardware.
papers.abstract: Machine learning accelerators present a unique set of design challenges across chip architecture, instruction set, server design, compiler, and both inter- and intra-chip connectivity. With AWS Trainium, we've utilized AWS's end-to-end ownership from chip to server, network, compilers, and runtime tools to collaboratively design and optimize across all layers, emphasizing simplicity and ease of use. This talk will illustrate the design principles, tradeoffs, and lessons learned during the development of three generations of AWS ML products, from conceptualization to placing systems in the hands of AWS customers.
papers.abstract_zh: 机器学习加速器在芯片架构、指令集、服务器设计、编译器以及芯片间和芯片内连接方面提出了一系列独特的设计挑战。借助 AWS Trainium，我们利用 AWS 的从芯片到服务器、网络、编译器和运行时工具的端到端所有权来跨所有层进行协作设计和优化，强调简单性和易用性。本次演讲将阐述三代 AWS ML 产品开发过程中的设计原则、权衡和经验教训，从概念化到将系统交付给 AWS 客户。

papers.title: BeeZip: Towards An Organized and Scalable Architecture for Data Compression.
papers.abstract:
Data compression plays a critical role in operating systems and large-scale computing workloads. Its primary objective is to reduce network bandwidth consumption and memory/storage capacity utilization. Given the need to manipulate hash tables, and execute matching operations on extensive data volumes, data compression software has transformed into a resource-intensive CPU task. To tackle this challenge, numerous prior studies have introduced hardware acceleration methods. For example, they have utilized Content-Addressable Memory (CAM) for string matches, incorporated redundant historical copies for each matching component, and so on. While these methods amplify the compression throughput, they often compromise an essential aspect of compression performance: the compression ratio (C.R.). Moreover, hardware accelerators face significant resource costs, especially in memory, when dealing with new large sliding window algorithms.

We introduce BeeZip, the first hardware acceleration system designed explicitly for compression with a large sliding window. BeeZip tackles the hardware-level challenge of optimizing both compression ratio and throughput. BeeZip offers architectural support for compression algorithms with the following distinctive attributes: 1) A two-stage compression algorithm adapted for accelerator parallelism, decoupling hash parallelism and match execution dependencies; 2) An organized hash hardware accelerator named BeeHash engine enhanced with dynamic scheduling, which orchestrates hash processes with structured parallelism; 3) A hardware accelerator named HiveMatch engine for the match process, which employs a new scalable parallelism approach and a heterogeneous scale processing unit design to reduce memory resource overhead.

Experimental results show that on the Silesia dataset, BeeZip achieves an optimal throughput of 10.42GB/s (C.R. 2.96) and the best C.R. of 3.14 (throughput of 5.95GB/s). Under similar compression ratios, compared to single-threaded/36-threaded software implementations, BeeZip offers accelerator speedups of 23.2×/2.45×, respectively. Against all accelerators we know, BeeZip consistently demonstrates a superior compression ratio, improving by at least 9%.
papers.abstract_zh:
数据压缩在操作系统和大规模计算工作负载中起着至关重要的作用。其主要目标是减少网络带宽消耗和内存/存储容量利用率。由于需要操作哈希表并在大量数据上执行匹配操作，数据压缩软件已转变为资源密集型 CPU 任务。为了应对这一挑战，许多先前的研究引入了硬件加速方法。例如，他们利用内容可寻址内存（CAM）进行字符串匹配，为每个匹配组件合并冗余历史副本，等等。虽然这些方法放大了压缩吞吐量，但它们通常会损害压缩性能的一个重要方面：压缩比 (C.R.)。此外，在处理新的大型滑动窗口算法时，硬件加速器面临着巨大的资源成本，特别是在内存方面。

我们推出 BeeZip，这是第一个专门为大滑动窗口压缩而设计的硬件加速系统。 BeeZip 解决了优化压缩比和吞吐量的硬件级挑战。 BeeZip 为压缩算法提供架构支持，具有以下独特属性： 1）适用于加速器并行性、解耦哈希并行性和匹配执行依赖性的两级压缩算法； 2）一个有组织的哈希硬件加速器，称为BeeHash引擎，增强了动态调度，它以结构化并行性编排哈希过程； 3）用于匹配过程的名为HiveMatch引擎的硬件加速器，它采用新的可扩展并行方法和异构规模处理单元设计来减少内存资源开销。

实验结果表明，在Silesia数据集上，BeeZip的最佳吞吐量为10.42GB/s（C.R. 2.96），最佳C.R.为3.14（吞吐量为5.95GB/s）。在相似的压缩比下，与单线程/36线程软件实现相比，BeeZip 的加速比分别为 23.2×/2.45×。与我们所知的所有加速器相比，BeeZip 始终表现出卓越的压缩比，提高了至少 9%。

papers.title: BitPacker: Enabling High Arithmetic Efficiency in Fully Homomorphic Encryption Accelerators.
papers.abstract:
Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables computing directly on encrypted data. Though FHE is slow on a CPU, recent hardware accelerators compensate most of FHE's overheads, enabling real-time performance in complex programs like deep neural networks. However, the state-of-the-art FHE scheme, CKKS, is inefficient on accelerators. CKKS represents encrypted data using integers of widely different sizes (typically 30 to 60 bits). This leaves many bits unused in registers and arithmetic datapaths. This overhead is minor in CPUs, but accelerators are dominated by multiplications, so poor utilization causes large area and energy overheads.

We present BitPacker, a new implementation of CKKS that keeps encrypted data packed in fixed-size words, enabling near-full arithmetic efficiency in accelerators. BitPacker is the first redesign of an FHE scheme that targets accelerators. On a state-of-the-art accelerator, BitPacker improves performance by gmean 59% and by up to 3×, and reduces energy by gmean 59%. BitPacker does not reduce precision and can be applied to all prior accelerators without hardware changes.
papers.abstract_zh:
完全同态加密 (FHE) 可以直接对加密数据进行计算。尽管 FHE 在 CPU 上速度很慢，但最新的硬件加速器补偿了 FHE 的大部分开销，从而在深度神经网络等复杂程序中实现实时性能。然而，最先进的 FHE 方案 CKKS 在加速器上效率低下。 CKKS 使用大小差异很大的整数（通常为 30 到 60 位）表示加密数据。这使得寄存器和算术数据路径中的许多位未使用。这种开销在 CPU 中很小，但加速器以乘法为主，因此利用率低会导致较大的面积和能量开销。

我们推出了 BitPacker，这是 CKKS 的一种新实现，它将加密数据打包在固定大小的字中，从而在加速器中实现接近完整的算术效率。 BitPacker 是针对加速器的 FHE 方案的首次重新设计。在最先进的加速器上，BitPacker 将性能提高了 gmean 59%，最高可达 3 倍，并将能耗降低了 gmean 59%。 BitPacker 不会降低精度，并且可以应用于所有先前的加速器，而无需更改硬件。

papers.title: Boost Linear Algebra Computation Performance via Efficient VNNI Utilization.
papers.abstract:
Intel's Vector Neural Network Instruction (VNNI) provides higher efficiency on calculating dense linear algebra (DLA) computations than conventional SIMD instructions. However, existing auto-vectorizers frequently deliver suboptimal utilization of VNNI by either failing to recognize VNNI's unique computation pattern at the innermost loops/basic blocks, or producing inferior code through constrained and rudimentary peephole optimizations/pattern matching techniques. Auto-tuning frameworks might generate proficient code but are hampered by the necessity for sophisticated pattern templates and extensive search processes.

This paper introduces a novel compilation methodology that generates high-performance VNNI-enabled code. By leveraging DLA's salient characteristics to identify VNNI's utilization opportunities, it proceeds to pinpoint the most effective strategies for feeding VNNI's inputs and hinding VNNI's execution latency via efficient memory access and register/cache reuse and exploited instruction-level parallelism. A tailored static cost analysis model guides this exploration, determining critical parameters for effective code transformation and generation. The evaluation on DLA and DNN workloads show that our framework outperforms state-of-the-art industrial compilers and research works.
papers.abstract_zh:
与传统的 SIMD 指令相比，英特尔的矢量神经网络指令 (VNNI) 在计算密集线性代数 (DLA) 时可提供更高的效率。然而，现有的自动矢量化器经常无法充分利用 VNNI，因为要么无法识别最内循环/基本块处的 VNNI 独特计算模式，要么通过受约束且基本的窥视孔优化/模式匹配技术生成较差的代码。自动调整框架可能会生成熟练的代码，但由于需要复杂的模式模板和广泛的搜索过程而受到阻碍。

本文介绍了一种新颖的编译方法，可生成支持 VNNI 的高性能代码。通过利用 DLA 的显着特征来确定 VNNI 的利用机会，它继续确定最有效的策略，通过高效的内存访问和寄存器/缓存重用以及利用指令级并行性来提供 VNNI 的输入并阻止 VNNI 的执行延迟。定制的静态成本分析模型指导这种探索，确定有效代码转换和生成的关键参数。对 DLA 和 DNN 工作负载的评估表明，我们的框架优于最先进的工业编译器和研究工作。

papers.title: Bounding Speculative Execution of Atomic Regions to a Single Retry.
papers.abstract:
Mutual exclusion has long served as a fundamental construct in parallel programs. Despite a long history of optimizing the lower-level lock and unlock operations used to enforce mutual exclusion, such operations largely dictate performance in parallel programs. Speculative Lock Elision, and more generally Hardware Transactional Memory, allow executing atomic regions (ARs) concurrently and speculatively, and ensure correctness by using conflict detection. However, practical implementations of these ideas are best-effort and, in case of conflicts, the execution of ARs is retried a predetermined number of times before falling back to mutual exclusion.

This work explores the opportunities of using cacheline locking to bound the number of retries of speculative solutions. Our key insight is that ARs that access exactly the same set of addresses when re-executing can learn that set in the first execution and execute non-speculatively in the next one by performing an ordered cacheline locking. This way the speculative execution is bounded to a single retry.

We first establish the conditions for ARs to be able to re-execute under a cacheline-locked mode. Based on these conditions, we propose cleAR, cacheline-locked executed AR, a novel technique that on the first abort, forces the re-execution to use cacheline locking. The detection and conversion to cacheline-locking mode is transparent to software.

Using gem5 running data-structure benchmarks and the STAMP benchmark suite, we show that the average number of ARs that succeed on the first retry grows from 35.4% in our baseline to 64.4% with cleAR, reducing the percentage of fallback (coarse-grain mutual exclusion) execution from 37.2% to 15.4%. These improvements reduce average execution time by 35.0% over a baseline configuration and by 23.3% over more elaborated approaches like PowerTM.
papers.abstract_zh:
长期以来，互斥一直是并行程序的基本结构。尽管优化用于强制互斥的较低级别锁定和解锁操作有着悠久的历史，但此类操作在很大程度上决定了并行程序的性能。推测性锁消除（更一般地说是硬件事务内存）允许并发且推测性地执行原子区域 (AR)，并通过使用冲突检测来确保正确性。然而，这些想法的实际实现是尽最大努力的，并且在发生冲突的情况下，AR 的执行会重试预定次数，然后再回退到互斥。

这项工作探讨了使用缓存行锁定来限制推测解决方案重试次数的机会。我们的主要见解是，在重新执行时访问完全相同的地址集的 AR 可以在第一次执行中了解该地址集，并通过执行有序的缓存行锁定在下一次执行中非推测性地执行。这样，推测执行仅限于一次重试。

我们首先建立 AR 能够在缓存行锁定模式下重新执行的条件。基于这些条件，我们提出了 cleAR（缓存行锁定执行 AR），这是一种新技术，在第一次中止时，强制重新执行使用缓存行锁定。检测和转换为高速缓存行锁定模式对于软件来说是透明的。

使用 gem5 运行数据结构基准测试和 STAMP 基准测试套件，我们发现第一次重试成功的 AR 平均数量从基线中的 35.4% 增长到 clearAR 的 64.4%，从而将回退（粗粒度互斥）执行的百分比从 37.2% 降低到 15.4%。这些改进使平均执行时间比基准配置减少了 35.0%，比 PowerTM 等更精细的方法减少了 23.3%。

papers.title: BVAP: Energy and Memory Efficient Automata Processing for Regular Expressions with Bounded Repetitions.
papers.abstract: Regular pattern matching is pervasive in applications such as text processing, malware detection, network security, and bioinformatics. Recent studies have demonstrated specialized in-memory automata processors with superior energy and memory efficiencies than existing computing platforms. Yet, they lack efficient support for the construct of bounded repetition that is widely used in regular expressions (regexes). This paper presents BVAP, a software-hardware co-designed in-memory Bit Vector Automata Processor. It is enabled by a novel theoretical model called Action-Homogeneous Non-deterministic Bit Vector Automata (AH-NBVA), its efficient hardware implementation, and a compiler that translates regexes into hardware configurations. BVAP is evaluated with a cycle-accurate simulator in a 28nm CMOS process, achieving 67-95% higher energy efficiency and 42-68% lower area, compared to state-of-the-art automata processors (CA, eAP, and CAMA), across a set of real-world benchmarks.
papers.abstract_zh: 常规模式匹配在文本处理、恶意软件检测、网络安全和生物信息学等应用中普遍存在。最近的研究表明，专用内存自动机处理器比现有计算平台具有更高的能源和内存效率。然而，它们缺乏对正则表达式（regexes）中广泛使用的有界重复构造的有效支持。本文介绍了 BVAP，一种软硬件协同设计的内存位向量自动机处理器。它是由一种称为动作同质非确定性位向量自动机 (AH-NBVA) 的新颖理论模型、其高效的硬件实现以及将正则表达式转换为硬件配置的编译器实现的。 BVAP 使用 28nm CMOS 工艺中的周期精确模拟器进行评估，在一组实际基准中，与最先进的自动机处理器（CA、eAP 和 CAMA）相比，能效提高了 67-95%，面积减少了 42-68%。

papers.title: BypassD: Enabling fast userspace access to shared SSDs.
papers.abstract:
Modern storage devices, such as Optane NVMe SSDs, offer ultra-low latency of a few microseconds and high bandwidth of multiple gigabytes per second. At these speeds, the kernel software I/O stack is a substantial source of overhead. Userspace approaches avoid kernel software overheads but face challenges in supporting shared storage without major changes to file systems, the OS or the hardware.

We propose a new I/O architecture, BypassD , for fast, userspace access to shared storage devices. BypassD takes inspiration from virtual memory: it uses virtual addresses to access a device and relies on hardware for translation and protection. Like memory-mapping a file, the OS kernel constructs a mapping for file contents in the page table. Userspace I/O requests then use virtual addresses from these mappings to specify which file and file offset to access. BypassD extends the IOMMU hardware to translate file offsets into device Logical Block Addresses. Existing applications require no modifications to use BypassD. Our evaluation shows that BypassD reduces latency for 4KB accesses by 42% compared to standard Linux kernel and performs close to userspace techniques like SPDK that do not support device sharing. By eliminating software overheads, BypassD improves performance of real workloads, such as the WiredTiger storage engine, by ~20%.
papers.abstract_zh:
现代存储设备（例如 Optane NVMe SSD）可提供几微秒的超低延迟和每秒数 GB 的高带宽。在这些速度下，内核软件 I/O 堆栈是开销的重要来源。用户空间方法避免了内核软件开销，但在支持共享存储而不对文件系统、操作系统或硬件进行重大更改方面面临着挑战。

我们提出了一种新的 I/O 架构 BypassD，用于在用户空间快速访问共享存储设备。 BypassD 从虚拟内存中获得灵感：它使用虚拟地址来访问设备，并依赖硬件进行转换和保护。就像内存映射文件一样，操作系统内核在页表中构造文件内容的映射。然后，用户空间 I/O 请求使用这些映射中的虚拟地址来指定要访问的文件和文件偏移量。 BypassD 扩展了 IOMMU 硬件，将文件偏移量转换为设备逻辑块地址。现有应用程序无需修改即可使用 BypassD。我们的评估表明，与标准 Linux 内核相比，BypassD 将 4KB 访问的延迟减少了 42%，并且性能接近不支持设备共享的 SPDK 等用户空间技术。通过消除软件开销，BypassD 将真实工作负载（例如 WiredTiger 存储引擎）的性能提高了约 20%。

papers.title: C4CAM: A Compiler for CAM-based In-memory Accelerators.
papers.abstract: Machine learning and data analytics applications increasingly suffer from the high latency and energy consumption of conventional von Neumann architectures. Recently, several in-memory and near-memory systems have been proposed to overcome this von Neumann bottleneck. Platforms based on content-addressable memories (CAMs) are particularly interesting due to their efficient support for the search-based operations that form the foundation for many applications, including K-nearest neighbors (KNN), high-dimensional computing (HDC), recommender systems, and one-shot learning among others. Today, these platforms are designed by hand and can only be programmed with low-level code, accessible only to hardware experts. In this paper, we introduce C4CAM, the first compiler framework to quickly explore CAM configurations and seamlessly generate code from high-level Torch-Script code. C4CAM employs a hierarchy of abstractions that progressively lowers programs, allowing code transformations at the most suitable abstraction level. Depending on the type and technology, CAM arrays exhibit varying latencies and power profiles.
